pacman::p_load(curl, 
               magrittr, 
               dplyr, 
               purrr, 
               glue, 
               readr, 
               #textTinyR,
               stringr
               #textAnnotatoR
               )

# Check for existing files matching the pattern
matching_files <- list.files(here::here("data"), pattern = file_pattern, full.names = TRUE)
Error in list.files(here::here("data"), pattern = file_pattern, full.names = TRUE) : 
  object 'file_pattern' not found
data3 <- data2 %>% 
  select(highlights.text, document.PublicationID) %>%
  mutate(text = stringr::str_c(highlights.text)) %>%
  select(-c(highlights.text)) %>%
  #dplyr::slice_(n=20) %>%
  #tidyr::unnest(highlights.text) %>%
  DataEditR::data_edit(x = .,
                       col_bind = "include",
                       col_stretch = F,
                       viewer = "pane",
                       code = T,
                       quiet = T)

Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
i In argument: `text = stringr::str_c(highlights.text)`.

! argument is not an atomic vector; coercing

"AE500639"), text = c("c(\"6 CONSULTATION AND CHANGE 6.1 This term applies if the Company: 6.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or 6.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees. 6.2 In relation to this clause the Company must notify the relevant Employees, and the Union if they so require, of the decision to introduce the major change as referred to in clause 6.1.1 or for a proposal to introduce a change as referred to in 6.1.2. 6.3 The relevant Employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 6.4 If: 6.4.1 a relevant Employee appoints, or relevant Employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; 6.4.2 the Employee or Employees advise the Company of the identity of the representative; and 6.4.3 the Union advises its intent to\", \n\"SCHEDULE 5: CLASSIFICATIONS LEVEL 1 Control room: Ship To Shore <strong>Automation</strong> Coordinator (fixed / <strong>Automated</strong> Stacking Crane <strong>Automation</strong> Coordinator (exception) Automatic Container Carrier waterside support coordinator Landside support coordinator Planning support coordinator (yard/vessel) Employees engaged in the above classification will, where required, also engage in tasks associated with the Company's <strong>automation</strong> process Ship: Shipside support coordinator (including <strong>Automated</strong> Lashing Platform exception management and lasher supervision) Maintenance: Entrance level trade certification.\", \n\"LEVEL 2 Control room: Designated Automatic Container Carrier waterside supervisor Designated Landside supervisor Designated Planning supervisor (yard/vessels) Designated Ship to Shore / Automatic Stocking Crane Trainer certified Employees engaged in the above classifications must also maintain and/or perform: . the skills of a Shift to Shore <strong>Automation</strong> Coordinator (fixed) / <strong>Automated</strong> Stacking Crane <strong>Automation</strong> Coordinator (exception); and where required, tasks associated with the <strong>automation</strong> process.\", \n\"Ship: Shipside supervisor (including <strong>Automated</strong> Lashing Platform exception management and lasher supervision + safety cert 4) Maintenance: Robotic specialist technician (Electrical/mechanical)) LEVEL 3 Control room: Designated operations shift lead Designated planning manager Designated landside manager Employees engaged in the above classifications must also maintain: the skills required for an Automatic Container Carrier waterside coordinator; the skills required for a Ship To Shore <strong>Automation</strong> Coordinator (fixed) / <strong>Automated</strong> Stacking Crane <strong>Automation</strong> Coordinator (exception); and where required, tasks associated with the <strong>automation</strong> process. 31 28318234v.6\", \n\"Ship: Maintenance ROBOTIC specialist TECHNICIAN (ELECTRICAL/MECHANICAL) SUPPLEMENTARY EMPLOYEES Lashing: Lashing Employees engaged in the above classification will, where required, engage in tasks associated with the <strong>automation</strong> process. 32 28318234v.6\")", 
"c(\"<strong>Automated</strong> Innovation Pty Limited Single Enterprise Agreement 2017 1.\", \"CONSULTATION TERM 13.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"<strong>Automated</strong> Innovation Pty Limited Single Enterprise Agreement 2017 7.\", \"<strong>Automated</strong> Innovation Pty Limited Single Enterprise Agreement 2017 4.\", \"<strong>Automated</strong> Innovation Pty Limited Single Enterprise Agreement 2017 7.\")", 
"c(\"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> Site Services Single Enterprise Agreement 2020\", \"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> 5.\", \"Consultation 8.1 This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> Site Services Single Enterprise Agreement 2020 Page 5 of 23\", \n\"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> 13.\", \"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> i.\")", 
"c(\"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> Site Services Single Enterprise Agreement 2023\", \"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> 27.\", \"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> 5.\", \"Consultation 8.1 This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> Site Services Single Enterprise Agreement 2023 Page 5 of 25\", \n\"SAGE <strong>Automation</strong> 13.\")", 
"c(\"CLAUSE 5: CONSULTATION 5.1 General 5.1.1 This clause applies if the Company: has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or · proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 5.1.2 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this clause.\", \n\"Seeley Technicians - Non Trade S Level Descriptor S1 Entry (Probation & Training) S2 Operator S3 Operator + S4 Operator ++ S5 Material Handler 1, Operator Setter, Line Coordinator, Warehouse, <strong>Automated</strong> Line Technician S6 Quality Coordinator, Material Handler 2 S7 Die Setter 1, Team Leader (in training), 2IC in Assembly Department S8 Die Setter 1+, Team Leader + S9 Die Setter 2, Team Leader ++ S10 Die Setter 2+ Definitions Training in home department and/or learning additional skills Operator in another department.\", \n\"Competent in home department and able to trouble shoot Operator ++ Robot <strong>Automation</strong> issues.\", \"<strong>Automated</strong> Line Operator who is fully competent in running all Robot Tech.\", \"<strong>Automated</strong> lines in a department and can trouble shoot and recover from <strong>automation</strong> issues.\")", 
"c(\"[2014] FWCA 4498 Annexure A Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"<strong>Automated</strong> train operations 11.\", \"The <strong>automation</strong> of the locomotives will be staged to occur in 3 different phases: Phase 1 - Rosella to the Coast .\", \"The consultative committee will not address the options or process for managing the impact on individual employees once <strong>automated</strong> trains are operating.\", \"The Company will consider recommendations made by the Operations Consultative Committee but can make decisions and implement actions in relation to <strong>automated</strong> train operation and retains the decision making authority in relation to autonomous train implementation. 11.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CLAUSE 5: CONSULTATION 5.1 General 5.1.1 This clause applies if the Company: . has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 5.1.2 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this clause.\", \n\"Seeley Technicians - Non Trade S Level Descriptor S1 Entry (Probation & Training) S2 Operator S3 Operator + $4 Operator ++ S5 Material Handler 1, Operator Setter, Line Coordinator, Warehouse, <strong>Automated</strong> Line Technician S6 Quality Coordinator, Material Handler 2 S7 Die Setter 1, Team Leader (in training), 2IC in Assembly Department S8 Die Setter 1+, Team Leader + S9 Die Setter 2, Team Leader ++ S10 Die Setter 2+ Definitions Training in home department and/or learning additional skills Operator in another department.\", \n\"Competent in home department and able to trouble shoot Operator ++ Robot <strong>Automation</strong> issues.\", \"<strong>Automated</strong> Line Operator who is fully competent in running all Robot Tech.\", \"<strong>Automated</strong> lines in a department and can trouble shoot and recover from <strong>automation</strong> issues.\")", 
"c(\"DELTA BUILDING <strong>AUTOMATION</strong> (QLD) PTY LIMITED - SINGLE ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT | 2014 1.\", \"The Company means DELTA BUILDING <strong>AUTOMATION</strong> (Qld) Pty Ltd (ABN 33 129 454 119).\", \"DELTA BUILDING <strong>AUTOMATION</strong> (QLD) PTY LIMITED - SINGLE ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2014 4.\", \"DELTA BUILDING <strong>AUTOMATION</strong> (QLD) PTY LIMITED - SINGLE ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2014 6.\", \"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION AND COMMUNICATION (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"PANDROL AUSTRALIA PTY LIMITED ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2014-2017 TECHNOLOGY The parties agree to the phased introduction of new processes such as but not confined to the <strong>Automated</strong> Scragging and Toe Insulator and E-Clip <strong>automation</strong>.\")",

"c(\"This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"PANDROL CLIP PLANT WORKPLACE AGREEMENT 2017 TECHNOLOGY The Parties agree to the phased introduction of new processes such as but not confined to the <strong>Automated</strong> Scragging and Toe Insulator and E-Clip <strong>automation</strong>.\")", 
"c(\".: AG2024/339 Applicant: National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd Section 185 - Application for approval of a single enterprise agreement Undertaking - Section 190 I, Anthony Fazzolari, Director of National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd, give the following undertakings with respect to the National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2024-2028 (\\\"the Agreement\\\"): 1.\", \"I have the authority given to me by National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd to provide this undertaking in relation to the application before the Fair Work Commission. 2.\", \n\".: AG2024/339 Applicant: National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd Section 185 - Application for approval of a single enterprise agreement Undertaking - Section 190 I, Anthony Fazzolari, Director of National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd, give the following undertakings with respect to the National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2024-2028 (\\\"the Agreement\\\"): 1 I have the authority given to me by National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd to provide this undertaking in relation to the application before the Fair Work Commission. 2.\"\n)",

"c(\"NATIONAL <strong>AUTOMATION</strong> CONTROLS PTY LTD ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2024-2028 This document sets out the proposed amendments to the National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2024-2028 (\\\"Agreement\\\").\", \"National <strong>Automation</strong> Controls Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2024-2028 17 ENDORSEMENT OF AGREEMENT 17.1 Signed for and on behalf of the Company Signed: Date: 23/02/24 Name in full (printed): ANTHONY FAZZOLARI Position title: DIRECTOR Address: 57 MARKET STREET, ESSENDON, VIC 3082 Witnessed by: Witness name in full: GAVIN SPRING Address: 82 HONOUR AVENUE, MACEDON, VIC 3440 17.2 Signed for and on behalf of the Employees Signed: Date: 23/02/24 Name in full (printed): ANTHONY NASTASI Position title: ELECTRICIAN Address: 1 MILES CLOSE, MILL PARK, VIC 3082 Witnessed by: Witness name in full: GAVIN SPRING Address: 82 HONOUR AVENUE, MACEDON, VIC 3440 27\"\n)",

"SIGNATORIES 24/11/15 Signed by: Date_ Full Name CHRIS CASSIDY 1 ARTHUR PHILLIP ORU ENDEAVOUR HUIS Address For and on behalf of Premiair <strong>Automation</strong> and Controls (ABN: 85165147866) Lapell 24/11/15 Witnessed by: Wayne Wasy Date: Full Name Signed on behalf of Employees of the Company: Signed by: 24/11/15 Date Simon Alger Full Name 8 The Boulevard, Montrose, 3765 Address For and on behalf of Premiair <strong>Automation</strong> and Controls (ABN: 85165147866) Wayneil. 24/11/15 Witnessed by Date : WAYNE WALSH Full Name", 
"c(\"SIEMENS Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd Port Melbourne Enterprise Agreement 2015 Issue: 1.0 Issue Date: Reference: Port Melbourne Enterprise Agreement 2015 Prepared: by: Michelle Veleski Human Resources Page 1 of 45\", \"TITLE This agreement shall be known as the Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd Port Melbourne Enterprise Agreement 2015, hereafter referred to as the \\\"Agreement\\\" 2.\", \"APPLICATION This agreement shall apply to all full time, part time and casual Employees of Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd, employed at the Employer's workplace at 46 Douglas Street, Port Melbourne and who are engaged in the classification structure set out in Clause 36.b - Classifications of this agreement. 3.\", \n\"Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd (the Employer); b.\", \"This is an agreement made between Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd (ABN 78 000 102 483) and all full time, part time and casual Employees engaged in the classification structure set out in Clause 36.b - Classifications.\")", 
"c(\"Decant product into the Goods to Person <strong>automation</strong> system 27\", \"Basic operation and staging of pallets from the <strong>automated</strong> wrap machine iv.\", \"(g) The Company or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: i. by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or il. if the Company and employee agree in writing - at any time. 6.4 CONSULTATION (a) This clause applies if the Company: 1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work == of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"SIEMENS Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd Site & Workshop Enterprise Agreement 2013 Issue: 1.0 Issue Date: 6/2/2014 Reference: Site & Workshop Enterprise Agreement 2013 Prepared: by: Michelle Sperring Human Resources Page 1 of 43\", \"TITLE This Agreement shall be known as the Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd Site & Workshop Rockhampton Enterprise Agreement 2013, (the \\\"Agreement\\\"). 2.\", \"Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd (the Employer); b.\", \"All employees of Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd who are described in Clause 2 - Application Agreement. 4.\", \n\"SIEMENS Siemens Rail <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd (Signature) JAMIE MCDyER.\")", 
"c(\"SIGNATORIES 38 APPENDIX 1 CLASSIFICATION STRUCTURE 40 APPENDIX 2: <strong>AUTOMATED</strong> ASSEMBLY EMPLOYEES WORKING A 24-7 ROSTER 41 3\", \"CONSULTATION TERM 17.1 This term applies if Dyno Nobel: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"For overtime, weekend and public holiday payments for <strong>Automated</strong> Assembly, see Appendix Two. 20.2 Overtime is all hours worked in excess of Ordinary Hours.\", \"On the basis of an Employee's Annualised Base Salary for <strong>Automated</strong> Assembly Employees. 27.9 Employees are entitled to unpaid carer's leave in accordance with the Act.\", \"APPENDIX 2: <strong>AUTOMATED</strong> ASSEMBLY EMPLOYEES WORKING A 24-7 ROSTER 1 The Company has the right to introduce new rosters or modify any existing roster.\"\n)", 
"c(\"COVERAGE OF AGREEMENT This Agreement applies to Toll Customised Solutions operational staff (Team Members) who are engaged in performing the following functions, but are not limited to: Goods receipt, picking (manual or <strong>automated</strong>) and replenishment, assembly, sortation and dispatch, loading and unloading tasks, inbound freight planning, fulfilment and production planning, operating MHE and activities supporting these functions in the distribution facility.\", \"OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY The Company reserves the right to introduce new technology, systems, processes and programs including but not limited to robotics/<strong>automation</strong>, new machinery, new equipment, warehousing software or computer systems which can contribute to increasing the operational productivity, efficiency and/or effectiveness of the business.\", \n\"This will also include safety <strong>automation</strong> to improve safe work practices.\")", 
"c(\"2 instalments of $104 (First pay 3065 208.00 208.00 per year IT14 miter 30th June & 31st Dec) 3145 0.00 15.00 Per Week IT14 3720 16.43 16.60 per day <strong>Automated</strong> 3766 0.68 0.72|cents <strong>Automated</strong> 5.\", \"To facilitate this, employees agree to: a) Show a safe and courteous driving manner to other road users; b) Ensure all chutes are free from residual concrete or slurry and prevented from falling onto roadway or other traffic in transit to or from site. ) Ensure that the truck & agitator are presented in a clean and tidy manner. 20 CONSULTATION CLAUSE 1) This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Lvl Classification Current Hourly Rate 1.8 % Increase WEEKLY RATE ANNUAL RATE G5 Driver In Charge 24.6634 0.4 73.1074 75 954 06 49.612.16 Driver Trainer 26.1310 0 47 RM 6013 1.010.85 52 .564 23 GE Semi Driver 25.1567 0.4 973 15 50,604.40 Wage Type Amount % Increase New Amount Units lapat via Comments 2 instalmonsta of $104 [First pay 3065 208.00 200.00 per je ar IT14 efter 300 June & 31st Decl 3145 0.00 15.00 Per Week IT14 3720 16.43 16.60 per day <strong>Automated</strong> 3766 0.68 0.72|cents <strong>Automated</strong> 5.\"\n)", 
"c(\"munications Electri ETU Plumbing Union MTL <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd and CEPU Electrical Division Queensland Enterprise Agreement 2023-2026 - 1-\", \"PART 1 - APPLICATION AND OPERATION 1.1 INTRODUCTION This Agreement has been negotiated between MTL <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd (the Company or the Employer), its Employees and the Union. 1.2 TITLE The title of this Agreement is the MTL <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd and CEPU Electrical Division Queensland Enterprise Agreement 2023 - 2026. 1.3 ARRANGEMENT OF AGREEMENT PART 1 - APPLICATION AND OPERATION 2 1.1 INTRODUCTION 2 1.2 TITLE .............. 2 1.3 ARRANGEMENT OF AGREEMENT 2 1.4 AGREEMENT OBJECTIVES 4 1.5 THE PARTIES. ......... ....... 4 1.6 DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 4 1.7 APPLICATION OF AGREEMENT ...... 7 1.8 DATE AND PERIOD OF OPERATION 7 1.9 AGREEMENT REVIEW. 7 PART 2 - COMMUNICATION AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION 8 2.1 DISPUTE SETTLEMENT PROCEDURE ... 2.2 UNION DELEGATE AND EMPLOYEE REPRESENTATIVES' RIGHTS ......... 8 2.3 FLEXIBILITY CLAUSE . 9 2.3A REQUESTS FOR FLEXIBLE WORKING ARRANGEMENTS 10 2.4 CONSULTATION - WORKPLACE CHANGE 12 PART 3 - EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP AND RELATED MATTERS ....... 13 3.1 EMPLOYMENT\", \n\"(k) Substantially reducing disputation and eliminating lost time due to disputation. 1.5 THE PARTIES The parties to this Agreement are: (a) MTL <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd (ACN/ABN 65 114 813 928 (b) Each and every Employee as defined in clause 1.6.1; and (c) The Electrical, Energy and Services Division of the Communications, Electrical, Electronic, Energy, Information, Postal, Plumbing and Allied Services Union of Australia, Queensland Branch (the CEPU). 1.6 DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 1.6.1 DEFINITIONS - General In this Agreement.\", \n\"2.4 CONSULTATION - WORKPLACE CHANGE 2.4.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"PART 11 - SIGNATORIES 11.1 SIGNATORIES Signed by: Date 2/2/2024 4 Full Name: LEWIS MCEWAN For and on behalf of MTL <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Ltd of 654 North Harcourt Road Sedgwick Victoria 3551 Position in Company: DIRECTOR.\")", 
"c(\"This Agreement applies to Toll Global Logistics operational staff (Team Members) who are perform the following functions, but are not limited to: (a) Goods-receipt, picking (manual or <strong>automated</strong>) and replenishment, assembly, sortation and dispatch, VAS kitting and labelling, inbound freight planning, fulfilment and production planning, operating MHE and activities supporting these functions in the distribution facility.\", \"Toll reserves the right to introduce new technology, systems, processes and programs including but not limited to robotics/<strong>automation</strong>, new machinery, new equipment, warehousing software or computer systems which can contribute to increasing the operational productivity, efficiency and/or effectiveness of the business.\", \n\"This will also include safety <strong>automation</strong> to improve safe work practices.\")", 
"c(\"Equipment maintenance including: Routine inspections, corrective maintenance and plant improvements on bagging and bulk loading equipment components and ancillaries Transport loading and finished product administration tasks Bulk loading including assistance to transport operators using the <strong>automated</strong> weighbridge loading system and off-bridge loading.\", \"Operates lifting equipment incidental to work within WHS guidelines Bulk loading diagnostics (including <strong>automated</strong> weighbridge system diagnostics and faultfinding) Operate semi-<strong>automated</strong> and <strong>automated</strong> packaging plant and in-line bagging plant operation, fault-finding and basic care.\", \n\"TRADE 6 Training, skill and knowledge standards required at Angaston for Training, skill and knowledge standards required at Angaston for personnel to fulfil the demands of Level 6 role requires the person personnel to fulfil the demands of Level 6 role requires the person to to fulfil all the requirements of the relevant Level 5 role and have the following additional competences outlined for the relevant fulfil all the requirements of the respective Level 5 role and have the following additional competences outlined for the relevant stream: stream: <strong>Automation</strong> & Control Stream: Carrying out Citect modifications/additions associated with Condition Monitoring Stream: maintenance related plant improvements and modifications · A minimum of 2 years' experience applying condition (excluding major projects) monitoring techniques, Citect program fault finding Applying advanced Condition Monitoring Techniques (i.e.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation regarding workplace change 12.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Spice room prebatching with full inventory and traceability Cycle counts and stock reconciliation Level 4 (92.4% An employee at Level 4 has: Competencies working Has met requirements of level 3 with competencies in relativity to the under general supervision one or more Level 3 Skill sets tradesperson) (i) completed an AQF Certificate 2 in Food but requires higher level of Kitchen cooking and ingredient preparation, product Processing; or Judgement transfers, and control of <strong>automated</strong> CIP (ii) has equivalent recognised enterprise or SAP receipt and transfers of materials, cycle counts and industrial experience, training or prior learning stock reconciliation and inventory control experience and/or skills to Level 4.\"\n)", 
"c(\"COVERAGE OF AGREEMENT This Agreement applies to Toll Customised Solutions operational staff (Team Members) who are engaged in performing the following functions, but are not limited to: Goods receipt, picking (manual or <strong>automated</strong>) and replenishment, assembly, sortation and dispatch and activities supporting these functions.\", \"OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY The Company reserves the right to introduce new technology, systems, processes and programs including but not limited to robotics/<strong>automation</strong>, new machinery, new equipment, warehousing software or computer systems which can contribute to increasing the operational productivity, efficiency and/or effectiveness of the business.\"\n)",

"c(\"(a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Advanced in RCS, <strong>Automation</strong> and HNS .\", \"Root Cause Analysis <strong>Automation</strong> Problem solving techniques Wide Range of Product and Machine Knowledge Investigates and solves complicated problems that need extensive analysis Suggests solutions that normally demand the use of new techniques/procedures/methods Initiate and run improvements and changes Safety for supervisors Contribute to SHEQ improvements in the workplace Supervisor Training Trade Assistant/Service person Level 1 .\"\n)", 
"c(\"(d) includes details of: (i) the terms of the Agreement that will be varied by the arrangement; and (ii) how the arrangement will vary the effect of the terms; and (iii) how the Employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of his or her employment as a result of the arrangement; and (e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. 3.4 Murray Goulburn will give the Employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 3.5 Murray Goulburn or Employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement (or such period as is prescribed by the FW Act); or (b) if Murray Goulburn and Employee agree in writing - at any time. 4 Consultation regarding major workplace change 4.1 This term applies if Murray Goulburn: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 4.2 For a major change referred to in paragraph 4.1(a): (a) Murray Goulburn must notify the relevant Employees and the NUW (or any other nominated representative) of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) Sub clauses 4.3 to 4.7 apply. 4.3 As soon as practicable after making its decision, Murray Goulburn must: (a) discuss with the relevant Employees and the NUW (or any other nominated representative): (i) the introduction of the change; and Murray Goulburn Co-operative Company Limited and National Union of Workers Devondale Dairy Beverage Centre Agreement 2016 Page 5\", \n\"APPENDIX A - CLASSIFICATIONS Classifications Production Technician Level 2 A Production Technician Level 2 will oversee the operations of multiple production lines and <strong>automated</strong> equipment in relation to the production of fresh milk.\", \"The key skills and experience of a Production Technician will include the following: · fault finding and troubleshooting; . strong mechanical aptitude; . working autonomously and as part of a team; high level computer literacy in relation to <strong>automated</strong> manufacturing software solutions.\"\n)",

basic computer skills <strong>automation</strong> experience\", \n\"Electrician - C9 An employee at this level has the following qualifications and experience: completed year 11 or equivalent . electrical trade qualification . relevant industrial apprenticeship experience . intermediate electrical aptitude intermediate computer skills <strong>automation</strong> experience Indicative tasks at this level include but are not limited to: . works under routine supervision either individually or in a team environment . is responsible for the quality of their own work subject to routine supervision exercises discretion within their level of skills and training .. assists in the provision of on the job training Fitter - C9 An employee at this level has the following qualifications and experience: completed year 11 or equivalent . mechanical fitter trade qualification relevant industrial apprenticeship experience basic mechanical aptitude basic fabrication and machining skills basic computer skills <strong>automation</strong> experience Indicative tasks at this level include but are not limited\", \n\"Fitter - C8 An employee at this level has the following qualifications and experience: . completed a mechanical fitter trade qualification relevant post trade courses relevant industrial apprenticeship experience basic mechanical aptitude basic computer skills . <strong>automation</strong> experience Indicative tasks at this level include, but are not limited to: Breakdown and preventative maintenance tasks . .\")", 
"c(\"Consultation Term Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 41.2.\", \"The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 41.3.\", \"Consultation Term Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This clause applies if the Company: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Maps and plans, Maintain manual and electronic record systems Operate computer systems and other <strong>automated</strong> equipment.\", \"Ability to operate relevant <strong>automated</strong> equipment and computer systems.\", \"An <strong>automated</strong> process will be run prior to the pay to provide a variance report identifying actual earnings in the pay cycle and a comparison to the relevant Award earnings.\", \"Also In relation to clause 4.1.1: \\\"The <strong>automated</strong> process described in clause 4.1.2.3 of the Agreement will also be run each pay cycle for day work employees whose ordinary hours finish after 6.00 pm.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation Term 5.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, 'organisation structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of the Employees.\", \n\"Employee Deductions 40.1 The Employer will provide a means through the Border Express Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.2 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.3 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 41.\")", 
"c(\"IVR - interactive Voice Recording this is the system you utilize to receive your start time the following day by calling <strong>automated</strong> number or using the IVR app on your phone.\", \"The following is the Model Consultation Clause which is the minimum requirement. 31.2 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"<strong>Automated</strong> Crossarm Cell Operation and associated tasks relevant to achieving overall equipment effectiveness and product performance Willing to undertake CERT IV in Leadership Ability to provide and report on efficiency and Right First Time results with interpretation through to relevant engineers and management Must be willing to attend to production requirements/queries after work hours to support a . 24 hour manufacturing operation Assist with Right First Time reporting and action strategies Investigate: Root Cause & Preventative Actions: Close-out WINS as required Maintain a current awareness of applicable legislation & guidelines regarding safe operation of plant Able to participate in Certification Audits Trouble shoot on right First Time cost strategies Organise and assist with training for other employees Implement Succession Planning strategies to ensure effective functionality of the site operations Must have a detailed understanding of the Wagners Safety, Quality and environmental\", \n\"O Willingness to take on other aspects of other roles when the situation requires, . . for example, during periods of team members annual leave Competent in Machine Start up and Shutdown (Pultrusion, <strong>automated</strong> and . . semiautomated Crossarm Cell Operations and Custom Build Machinery) Fills in for Maintenance Supervisor on a rostered or permanent basis .\", \"Competently operates <strong>Automated</strong> plant . .\", \"Be able to perform the role of <strong>Automated</strong> Crossarm Machine Operator or Plant .\"\n)", 
"c(\"Level 1 Employees who have completed 6 months probationary period or who is engaged at level 1 by the business Functions Assembly of components Repetition work either manual or semi - automatic Use of all hand tools Movement of all material by hand Level 2 Employee who has been assessed and deemed competent in all level one functions and who is required to perform work at this level Level 3 Employees Functions As per level 2 Cutting glass either manually or <strong>automated</strong> Forklift and crane driving Despatching duties Truck driving Level 4 Employees having completed a flat glass apprenticeship Employees with relevant trade qualification Employees assessed as being at this level but with no formal qualification Level 5 An employee who has been assessed or otherwise qualifies for Level 4 or other employee who takes on duties that require control of functions or areas.\", \n\"CONSULTATIVE PROCESSES (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees, (2) Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) The employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) Subclauses (3) - (9) apply (3)The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. (4) If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise the representative. (5) As soon as practicable after making its decision, the employer\", \n\"<strong>Automated</strong> cutting machine, b.\")", 
"c(\"Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 5.1.2.\", \n\"rate of time and one-half for the first two hours and double time thereafter to employees covered by this agreement for: 10.4.1 All time worked in excess of the ordinary hours of work in any week (40 hours per week); 10.5 In calculation of overtime, portions of hour shall be taken to the nearest one-tenth of an hour. 10.6 An employee required to work on a Saturday shall: 10.6.1 be paid at the rate of time and one-half for the first two (2) hours and double time thereafter for all time worked, with a minimum payment of four (4) hours at the appropriate rate of pay, whether the employee works for that period of time or not. 10.7 An employee required to work on a Sunday shall: 10.7.1 be paid at the rate of double time for all time worked, with a minimum payment of four (4) hours at the appropriate rate of pay, whether the employee works for that period or not. 10.8 The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong>\", \n\"deductions from their weekly pay. 10.9 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 10.10 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 11.\")", 
"c(\"CALLIDUS WELDING SOLUTIONS WORKSHOP ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2024 Classification Hourly Rates Permanent (gross) Casual (gross) (inclusive of 25% \\\"casual loading) Supervisor $67.30 $84.13 Team Leader $60.54 $75.67 Welder/Fabricator/Fitter/Machinist Level 1 $53.78 $67.23 Welder/Fabricator/Fitter/Machinist Level 2 $47.19 $58.99 Welder/Fabricator/Fitter/Machinist Level 3 $40.14 $50.18 <strong>Automation</strong> Level 1 $44.03 $55.03 <strong>Automation</strong> Level 2 $35.62 $44.52 <strong>Automation</strong> Level 3 $30.33 $37.91 Technical/Trades Assistant Level 1 $38.77 $48.47 Technical/Trades Assistant Level 2 $35.62 $44.52 Technical/Trades Assistant Level 3 $30.33 $37.91 Apprentices 8.2 Apprentices will be engaged in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and relevant apprenticeship legislation and/or regulations made by any State or Territory training authority.\", \n\"CONSULTATION 33.1 This Clause applies if the Company: (a) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Appendix 1 Domestic Site Day Rate/Night Rate 1.1 This day rate/night rate schedule has been calculated in accordance with Clause 11 of the Agreement. 1.2 For work performed by an Employee outside of the Perth Metropolitan Area at a client site they will receive the following rates: Classification Day Shift Night Shift Team Leaders/Supervisors $1,070.00 $1,070.00 Welder/Fabricator/Fitter/Machinist Level 1, 2 & 3 $885.00 $885.00 <strong>Automation</strong>/Technical/Trade Assistants Level 1, 2 &3 $700.00 $700.00 Apprentices 1.3 The rates stipulated above will remain the same for the course of the Agreement and will not be subject to any increases. 1.4 Superannuation will be paid in accordance with Clause 12 of the Agreement. 1.5 The hours of work expected on site are up to 13 hours per day for Team Leaders/Supervisors and 12 hours per day for all levels below this. 1.6 The Company will pay travel time as prescribed in the relevant mobilisation document at the Employee's Base Hourly Rate. 1.7 Where an Employee\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 11.1 Major Change a) For a major change referred to in Clause 11.a): the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and ii. subclauses b) to h) apply. b) The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. c) If: i. a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and ii. the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; iii. the employer must recognise the representative. d) As soon as practicable after making its decision, the employer must\", \n\"The tasks performed are largely <strong>automated</strong> and set protocols are followed.\", \"Staff however will be required to have: · A diploma or undergraduate degree Technical Competency Required Must obtain 6 points Supervision Received Close Supervision provided to others None Knowledge Basic Work Complexity <strong>Automated</strong> and Straightforward Judgement and Problem Solving Limited Written Communication Sound Patient Contact None Continuous Education (APACE or equivalent Not required scheme) 39\", \n\"May cross over into another laboratory where tasks performed are largely <strong>automated</strong> and set protocols are followed.\", \"Staff however will be required to have: University undergraduate degree with a science basis Technical Competency Required Must Obtain 12 points Supervision Received Close to routine Supervision provided to others Level 1 and Level 2 technicians/assistants Knowledge Routine Work Complexity <strong>Automated</strong> and Straightforward Judgement and Problem Solving Limited to routine Written Communication Sound Patient Contact None Continuous Education (APACE or equivalent Not required scheme) 40\"\n)", 
"c(\"From 30 Juno 22 this salary includes a makenip amount as compen sation to hiove the increase from 2 May to 30 June and a moll in of (axi and joining and leaving allowances ULA - 51,900), which will no longer be paid as separate allowances. 10.8 Leave ratio for TIR's is 0.33333, based on 2 weeks leave for each 6-week period of work. 11 CONSULTATION 11.1 This term applies if the employer a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation. structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees Major change 11.2 For a major change referred to in paragraph ( f)(a): a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to Introduce the major change; and bj subclauses [3] to (9) apaly. 11.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in\", \n\"Nothing in the foregoing shall infringe upon the right of the Company to make lawful decisions Le , the right to manage. 12.7 Where a vessel is required to call at ports where <strong>automated</strong> mooring facilities are not available the parties agree to consult in relation to the required manning.\", \"It is agreed that the manning levels applied as a result of the introduction of <strong>automated</strong> mooring arrangement will represent the agreed minimum manninig level of Chief Integrated Rating. 3 x Integrated Ratings, Chief Cook and Chief Caterer for operations in those ports 12.8 Where a vessel is in dry dock, and a crew member has to be repatriated, a replacement would not be sent to the vessel until immediately prior to departure from the port.\"\n)", 
"c(\"RETURN OF PROPERTY 51.7 Upon termination of employment for any reason Employees must return immediately all property belonging to the Company. 51.8 Any outstanding property of the Company not returned, or costs/moneys owed by an Employee to the Company, will be deducted from the final payment of moneys made to the Employee. 52.0 CONSULTATION 52.1 This term applies if the Company: ) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 52.2 Major change Page 15 of 21 Metso Outotec Australia Limited (Canning Vale Maintenance Division) and AMWU Enterprise Agreement 2023\", \n\"drawing Use comparison and basic measuring devices MEM 18002B Use power tools/hand held operations M.3 - Trade Qualified - Probation Required Competencies for Level M2 Per Core Role Competencies for Levels M4 or M5 Required Competencies for Level M2 CPCCLRG3001A Licence to perform rigging basic level Required Working at heights licence 1 Elevated work platform licence MEM050110 Assemble fabricated components MEM 16011A Communicate with individuals and small groups ME M16008A Interact with computing technology IEM 16012A IEM12024A Interpret technical specifications and manuals Required Perform computations JEM03002B Perform precision assembly MEM14004A Plan to undertake a routine task MEM09202A Produce free hand sketches MEM 18006C Repair and fit engineering components VEM16014A Report technical information MEM180119 Shut down and solate machines equipment 1EM05010C Apply fabrication, forming and shaping techniques Preferred MEM18014B Manufacture press tools and gauges MEMO5009C Perform <strong>automated</strong>\", \n\"5 - Trade Qualified Maintenance Worker Requis CPCCLRG3001A 1Licence to perform ringing basic level Required Working at heights licence onlined spaces licence Elevated work platform licence Required WIEM1050100 Apply fabrication, forming and shaning techniques MEM05011D Assemble fabricated components MEM 10006B Install machine / plant MEM 10010B EM 16012A Install pipework and pipework assemblies interpret technical specifications and manuals MIEM 18004B 1EM18024B Maintain and overhaul mechanical equipment M18021B Maintain engine cooling systems EM18019B Maintain hydraulic systems Maintain pneumatic systems TEM13007B Maintain water treatment systems for cooling towers MEM18014B Manufacture press tools and gauges MD9203A MEMO7 Measure and sketch site information Operate and monitor intermediate class boiler IEN DO9C Perform <strong>automated</strong> thermal cutting TEM18010C Perform equipment condition monitoring and recording MEM18012B Perform installation and removal of mechanical seals MEMO5013C Perform\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"APPENDIX A - CLASSIFICATIONS Classifications Production Technician Level 2 A Production Technician Level 2 will oversee the operations of multiple production lines and <strong>automated</strong> equipment in relation to the production of fresh milk.\", \"The key skills and experience of a Production Technician will include the following: (i) fault finding and troubleshooting; (ii) strong mechanical aptitude; (iii) working autonomously and as part of a team; (iv) high level computer literacy in relation to <strong>automated</strong> manufacturing software solutions.\"\n)", 
"c(\"FITNESS, READINESS AND PREPARATION FOR WORK 2.5.1 Employees are required to attend work being fit and ready. 2.5.2 (a) At commencement of work shift - the Employee must be appropriately dressed in the required company-issued protective clothing prior to logging in via the <strong>automated</strong> computer time keeping system and their shift commencement.\", \"(b) At the completion of the work shift - the Employee must log out via the <strong>automated</strong> computer time keeping system prior to removing company issued protective clothing and leaving the building.\", \n\"PART THREE: CONSULTATION AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION 3.1 CONSULTATION ABOUT WORKPLACE CHANGE. 3.1.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"(b) Each Employee will receive a payslip, which complies with the requirements of the Fair Work Regulations 2009 (Cth) (c) Employees are required to use an <strong>automated</strong> time recording system to ensure the timely and accurate payment of wages.\", \"Training in the use of the <strong>automated</strong> recording system will be provided upon commencement of employment.\")", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"It is expected that employees engaged at Level A are skilled & competent across multiple machines or equipment of a different nature - (Optical sorters, specialised cutting equipment, wash line and driers, <strong>automated</strong> cooking equipment, FFS <strong>automated</strong> packing equipment, linear <strong>automated</strong> pack lines, foreign material detection equipment, <strong>automated</strong> pallet jacks and fork hoists) and/or competent in skilled duties in multiple areas of the site, in the Level A range.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Any such costs associated will, where agreed by the Company and the Consultative Committee, be borne by the Company. 4.2.7 The processes set out in this Clause may include reference to the training needs of the Employees and the Company. 4.2.8 This Agreement does not require the Employer to provide any relevant training committee with information about Employees such as names of Employees, addresses, classifications and records that the Employer Is required to keep under the Act. 4.3 CONSULTATION TERM 4.3.1 General (a) This term applies if the Company: (1) has made a definite decision to Introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>In</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>Is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (11) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees. 4.3.2 Major Change (a) For a major change referred to in paragraph 4.3.1(a)(i): (1) the Company must notify the relevant Employees\", \n\"Measurement will be from <strong>automated</strong> productivity reports from MXP - showing number of completed jobs and number of uncompleted jobs. 2.2.\", \"Measurement will be from <strong>automated</strong> productivity reports from MXP - showing number of completed jobs and number of uncompleted jobs. 2.3.\", \"Completed jobs per day Minimum Standard = Achieve average: 20 points per day for each day worked 100 points per week 400 points per month KPI Bonus Achleve average: 25 points per day for each day worked 125 points per week 500 points per month (average 20 day month) Measurement will be from <strong>automated</strong> productivity reports from MXP - showing number of completed jobs and number of uncompleted jobs. 1.2.\"\n)",

than required at level C Performs operations requiring basic skills to fault find complex PLC , VSD , safety control systems and <strong>automation</strong> processes Repairs and maintains, tests, modifies or fault finds on machinery which utilises but not limited to mechanical , hydraulic and/or pneumatic principles and in the course of such work, is required to read and understand electronic, a Diploma of Engineering.\", \n\"Has achieved at least an 85% score in the Maintenance Skills Matrix Performs purchasing functions at a competent standard within prescribed authority limits Exercises high precision trade skills using various materials and/or specialised techniques Installs, repairs and maintains, tests, modifies, commissions and/or fault finds on complex machinery which utilises systems above that is required of level D Works on complex or intricate circuitry which involves examining, diagnosing and modifying systems comprising of interconnected circuits Mechanical Stream Employees · Specialised fabrication tasks using multiple engineering techniques (e.g. combination of machining and welding skills) higher than that required and level D Performs operations requiring
