"c(\"Level 2 Employee who has been assessed and deemed competent in all level one functions and who is required to perform work at this level Level 3 Employees as per level 2 Functions Cutting glass either manually or <strong>automated</strong> Forklift and crane driving Despatching duties Truck driving Level 4 .\", \"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR CHANGE (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation Term 6.1 This term applies if the Employer: 6.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or 6.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Employee Deductions 39.1 The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 39.2 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 39.3 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 40.\")", 
"c(\"Level 1 Employees who have completed 6 months probationary period or who is engaged level 1 by the business Functions 0 Assembly of components Repetition work either manual or semi-automatic Use of all hand tools Movement of all material by hand Level 2 Employee who has been assessed and deemed competent in all level one functions and who is required to perform work at this level Level 3 Employees as per level 2 Functions Cutting glass either manually or <strong>automated</strong> .\", \"(a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees (2) Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (1 )(a): (a) The employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) Subclauses (3)- (9) apply (3) The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. (4) If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise the representative. (5) As soon as practicable after making its decision, the employer\", \n\"Semi -<strong>Automated</strong> cutting machine, b.\")", 
"c(\"(b) The time off shall be taken at times that are convenient to the Employer after consultation with the employee. 2.4 CONSULTATION 2.4.1 This clause applies if: (a) the Employer has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or F:Stop Studio Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2014 Page 5 of 20\", \n\"Take customer orders for videos or photos; Assist customers with the operation of the <strong>automated</strong> ordering system and selection of photos; Handling cash and operating EFTPOS facilities; Assist higher level employees with any reasonably requested task; F:Stop Studio Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2014 Page 19 of 20\")", 
"c(\"(d) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"(f) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"CHS 18 Introduction of Major Change in the Workplace 19.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"(d) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"(f) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"(b) The Employer and: i. an Employee; ii. the majority of Employees who are employed on a particular date; or iii. the majority of Employees who are rostered to work on a particular shift; may agree to substitute another part-day for a part-day that would otherwise be a part-day public holiday under the NES. 18 Introduction of Major Change in the Workplace 18.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: 40.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 40.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Appropriate licence to operate required materials handling equipment and/or container handling equipment and/or crane, with capacity rated greater than 20,000 kg (as required), Knowledge of operation of fully <strong>automated</strong> core line operation. . .\", \"The following tasks are indicative of the tasks which an employee at this level may be required to perform: Operator in charge of a fully <strong>automated</strong> core line operation ..\")", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: 40.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 40.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Knowledge of operation of fully <strong>automated</strong> core line operation.\", \"Operator in charge of a fully <strong>automated</strong> core line operation .\")", 
"c(\"Consultation Term 5.1. term applies if the employer: 5.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; and 5.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"The Employer shall provide a means through the Border Express Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.2.\", \"The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.3.\")", 
"c(\"Any such costs associated will, where agreed by the Company and the Consultative Committee, be borne by the Company. 4.2.7 The processes set out in this Clause may include reference to the training needs of the Employees and the Company. 4.2.8 This Agreement does not require the Employer to provide any relevant training committee with information about Employees such as names of Employees, addresses, classifications and records that the Employer is required to keep under the Act. 4.3 CONSULTATION TERM 4.3.1 General (a) This term applies if the Company: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees. 4.3.2 Major Change (a) For a major change referred to in paragraph 4.3.1(a)(i): (i) the Company must notify the relevant Employees\", \n\"Measurement will be from <strong>automated</strong> productivity reports from MXP - showing number of completed jobs and number of uncompleted jobs. 2.2.\", \"Completed jobs per day Minimum Standard = Achieve average: 20 points per day for each day worked 100 points per week 400 points per month KPI Bonus = Achieve average: 25 points per day for each day worked 125 points per week - 500 points per month (ave 20 day month) Measurement will be from <strong>automated</strong> productivity reports from MXP - showing number of completed jobs and number of uncompleted jobs. 1.2.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Discipline for underperformance 3.1 For employees who are paid by piecework rates and engaged in the harvesting of mushrooms; (a) Upon completion of the mandatory six-month period of probation, PVM has benchmark minimum and target picking rate standards which are reflected in PVM's Picking Speed Policy for all harvest employees who pick mushrooms using <strong>automated</strong> hydraulic picking trolleys; (b) The relevant standard is compared to the average picking rate for each week over varying periods of consecutive weeks for all harvest employees ; (c) Harvest employees are initially employed on a trial and probationary basis with two assessment milestones (i) an initial two-week employment trial period in which the Harvest Manager must determine whether the candidate employee has a reasonable prospect of becoming a competent harvester or otherwise, If it is unequivocally clear that the applicant will not achieve the relevant harvesting benchmark rate, there will be no formal offer of employment and\", \n\"If, however the employee fails to achieve minimum picking rate standard when using an <strong>automated</strong> hydraulic picking trolley in the last four weeks of the probationary period, PVM may issue a notice of termination at its sole discretion; (d) Following the end of the probationary period, in circumstances where a harvester's weekly average remains below benchmark minimum picking rate standards referred to in 4.1 (a) above, for any six consecutive weeks, PVM reserves the right to (i) undertake an evaluation of the harvester's performance at any time; and (ii) undertake a verbal discussion with the employee to counsel them about deficiencies in their performance; and . agree actions to improve performance including the offer of additional training to improve the employee's picking speed; (e) In the absence of any improvement over the following six consecutive weeks, a further evaluation will be conducted, further assistance offered and a first warning letter issued (f) In the absence of any improvement\", \n\"This term applies if the employer: 1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if: 5.1.1 the Employer has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; and 5.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of the Employees.\", \n\"Employee Deductions 40.1 The Employer shall provide a means through the Border Express Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.2 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.3 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 41.\")", 
"c(\"Industrial <strong>Automation</strong> and Control Allowance. 21 58.\", \"Consultation 45.1 This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Industrial <strong>Automation</strong> and Control Allowance 57.1 To where an employee becomes formally qualified in Industrial <strong>Automation</strong> and Control (Certificate 3) and is appointed in writing in an Industrial <strong>Automation</strong> and Control role they shall be paid an allowance of $2.70 per hour in addition to the classification rate they are already paid. 58.\")", 
"c(\"(d) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"(f) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"Where a part-time or full-time Employee is usually rostered to work ordinary hours on the declared or prescribed part-day public holiday but as a result of being on annual leave does not work, they will be taken not to be on annual leave during the hours of the declared or prescribed part-day public holiday that they would have usually been rostered to work and will be paid their ordinary rate for such hours; iv. where an Employee works any hours on the declared or prescribed part-day public holiday they will be entitled to the appropriate public holiday penalty rate (if any) in this Agreement for those hours worked. 19 Introduction of Major Change in the Workplace 19.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 9.1 This Clause 9 applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or Epiroc Burnie Service Centre Enterprise Agreement 2023 6\", \n\"Appendix 1 - Classifications Service Technician Level Criteria Level 0 Competent Trades Person Level 1 1 Heavy Plant or Auto Electrical Trade Qualification Service Level 1 and Service Level 2 internal training completed Training on at least one Product/Machine in local branch Works within in settled limits Identifies and solves easy problems Understands the use of available techniques/procedures/ methods Level 2 Heavy Plant or Auto Electrical Trade Qualification Service Level 1 and Service Level 2 internal training completed Training on most Products/Machines in the local branch Identifies and solves most frequently upcoming problems Works according to available techniques/procedures/methods Contributes to improvements and change in work area Level 3 (Leading Hand, Product Support Technician) Heavy Plant or Auto Electrical Trade Qualification Service Level 1 and Service Level 2 internal training completed Root Cause Analysis <strong>Automation</strong> Machine/Product Training on most Epiroc machines Investigates\", \n\"and solves complicated problems Contributes to the development of techniques/procedures/methods Contributes actively to improvements and changes in the workplace Basic supervision Level 4 Heavy Plant or Auto Electrical Trade Qualification Service Level 1 and Service Level 2 internal training completed Root Cause Analysis <strong>Automation</strong> Problem solving techniques Wide Range of Product and Machine Knowledge Investigates and solves complicated problems that need extensive analysis Suggests solutions that normally demand the use of new techniques/procedures/methods Initiate and run improvements and changes Safety for supervisors Contribute to SHEQ improvements in the workplace Supervisor Training Epiroc Burnle Service Centre Enterprise Agreement 2023 22\"\n)", 
"c(\"Payment of 1 hour will also apply for the use of the <strong>automated</strong> Sydney Truck Wash.\", \"Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. (2) For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (3) to (9) apply. (3) The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. (4) If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise the representative. (5) As soon as practicable\"\n)", 
"c(\"All Employees will be provided with Personal Protective Equipment for the role in the Production Department, 2.3 CONSULTATION (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees, Major change (2) For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (3) to (9) apply. (3) The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. (4) If.\", \n\"Improved efficiency and daily output - 33% of available bonus Quality standards and reduction in defective parts - 34 % of available bonus Attendance, absenteeism and lost time at work - 33% of available bonus Efficiency - measured monthly - average of 6 monthly totals Standard times (cycle time) have been calculated for all work areas per model, based on current processes and levels of <strong>automation</strong> (see below).\")", 
"c(\"(d) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"(f) Basic understanding and operation of pick technology including but not limited to RF, voice and <strong>automation</strong>.\", \"(b) The Employer reserves the right to require an Employee who needs to take (or needed to take) personal leave (paid or unpaid) to submit a medical certificate or statutory declaration for that personal leave. 17 Other Leave Employees are entitled to long service leave in accordance with the New South Wales Long Service Leave legislation and Parental leave in accordance with the National Employment Standards. 18 Public Holidays 18.1 Permanent Employees Permanent Employees are entitled to Public Holidays in accordance with the National Employment Standards. 19 Introduction of Major Change in the Workplace 19.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 7.2.\", \n\"Employee Deductions 42.4 The employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay 42.5 The employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 42.6 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 43.\")",

"c(\"5.7 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery.\", \"dispute using the procedures in this term: (A) an employee must continue to perform his or her work as he or she would normally unless he or she has a reasonable concern about an imminent risk to his or her health or safety; and (B) an employee must comply with a direction given by the employer to perform other available work at the same workplace, or at another workplace, unless: (i) the work is not safe; or (ii) applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or (iii) the work is not appropriate for the employee to perform; or (iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. (6) The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 9 Consultation regarding major workplace change (a) This term applies if the employer: (1) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (2) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\")", 
"c(\"Consultation Term 7.1 This term applies if the Employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Employee Deductions 42.1 The Employer will provide a means through the Company Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 42.2 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 42.3 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 43.\")", 
"c(\"Consultation Term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> ... <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or Border Express Pty Ltd Transport and Distribution FacilityEmployees South Australia Agreement 2014-2017 Draft - 11.\", \n\"The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 41.2.\", \"The Employer. will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 41.3.\")", 
"c(\"Consultation Term 7.1 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Monsbent EBA Positions Tasks Basic Intermediate Advanced Drymill Operator Operate Kilns 10 15 20 Kombi Operator Operate MachInery in Drymill 10 15 20 Greenmill Operator Operate Machinery In Greenmill 10 15 20 Logistics Operator Operate Machinery in Kombi 10 15 20 Kiln Operator Operate Forklift 10 15 20 Loader Operator Operate Machinery in Woodmizer 10 15 20 Woodmizer Operator Operate Saw Shop Equipment 10 15 20 Evaluator Operator Hewsaw Operator Saw Doctor Buddy 5 10 Leading Hand 20 Trade Qual Relevant to Role 10 15 20 Pack Docker 10 Twin Edger 5 First Aid 10 Operate <strong>Automated</strong> Strapper 5 Quality Control 10 15 OH&S 5 Warden Trained 10 Fire Trained 5 Chainsaw 5 Level 1 2 3 4 0 - 75 76 - 100 101 - 125 126+ 5.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 8.1 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Smoking on the site is prohibited. 10.4 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the worksite during work times, as this can effect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 10.5 Tool Issue All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\")", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: 5.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 5.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from thelr weekly pay. 40.2.\", \"The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 40.3.\")", 
"c(\"REMUNERATION - PRODUCTION WORKERS CYLINDER FILLERS AND INSTALLATIONS FITTERS 8.1 An <strong>automated</strong> time and attendance system applies. 8.2 Production Workers and Cylinder Fillers starting at or after 3:00am on the first weekday after a break will receive their normal hours pay plus an additional 50% loading until 5:00am, where work performed is part of the ordinary hours of work.\", \"WORKPLACE CONSULTATION 33.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Reports any safety concerns, issues with quality and non-conformance of product Level 4 - <strong>Automated</strong> Operator Performs all duties of Level 3 Machine Operator Trouble shoots machinery breakdowns and can identify when Maintenance assistance is required Leads all product change overs Responsible for the set up of inclusion feeders and the setting up of Ripple machine Regularly liaises with shift supervisor regarding production plan Manages the work flow with Pasteurising operators, helps manage the tank allocation/ flavour mix and flavouring process Experienced churn operator Operates and troubleshoots Catta <strong>automated</strong> machinery Operates and troubleshoots Case packer Operates and troubleshoots the Robot process Communicates well across the team and all other departments Demonstrates awareness and records waste, and contributes ideas for reduction of waste Performs work with minimal supervision Level 4B - Store Person: Dry store Valid forklift license .\", \n\"with bag and box duties Assist with ripple manufacture Complete all Colour and Flavour duties Follow all Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) for area Follow all GMP / OHS site policies Identifies and reports any safety concerns and hazards Assists in provision of on the job training Level 5 Team Leader - Production Experience in a supervisory role and building a team Has sound manufacturing experience and able to troubleshoot and implement process improvements Excellent communications skills and can lead a diverse workgroup Labour planning and rostering Supervision and direction of production staff Training and development of production staff Executes daily production plan Leads changeover process on production line Maintains focus on safety Works closely with all other departments and all team leaders Attends weekly production meetings Helps implement improvement projects Supervision and direction of production staff Able to perform all duties of Level 3 Machine Operator and Level 4 <strong>Automated</strong>\", \n\"APPENDIX B MODEL CONSULTATION TERM (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. (2) Major Change - For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (3) to (9) apply. (3) The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term if: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise the representative. (4) As soon as practicable after making\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE (1)This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"As part of Cement Australia's continuous improvement program, the Employer will introduce an <strong>automated</strong> and auditable time capture system for Employee drivers in Victoria.\")", 
"c(\"introduction of new technology 0 changes to work organisation integration of acquired businesses . quality productivity improvement . new management practices 18.1.3 Employee representatives on the committee will have adequate time and access to the persons they represent prior to the Committee meetings to prepare for agenda items and following Committee meetings to report back, when necessary, on issues discussed. 18.1.4 Meetings will be held at least every 8 weeks. 18.1.5 In addition to the consultative committee, a Safety, Health and Environment Committee is also established in accordance with the provisions of this clause. 18.2 Consultation regarding major workplace change 18.2.1 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster\", \n\"Demonstrates ability to operate <strong>automated</strong> machinery including functionality of programmable logic controllers (PLC) Demonstrates ability to prepare production lines for operation e.g. start up of power, air supply, adjust conveyor heights, change labels, and heating elements.\")", 
"c(\"d) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. 5.7 The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 6 Consultation Term 6.1 This term applies if the employer: 6.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 6.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"The Employer agrees to provide training to Employees in connection with the introduction of any new technology. 38.5 Any reference to training in this clause excludes training, howsoever described, that is provided by a trade union. 39 Employee Deductions 39.1 The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 39.2 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee's to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. . - 39.3 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 40 Negotiation of Next Agreement 40.4 The parties agree to commence negotiation on the next Enterprise Agreement no less than three calendar months prior to the expiry of this Agreement. 40.5 Either party may provide a minimum four weeks' notice in writing to the other party should they not be able to commence bargaining due to circumstances\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation Term 6.1 This term applies if the Employer: 6.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or 6.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Employee Deductions 39.1 The Employer shall provide a means through the Company Employee Self-Serve (ESS) System through which the Employee may make <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 39.2 The Employer will provide the relevant training and assistance to Employee to manage <strong>automated</strong> deductions from their weekly pay. 39.3 Employees are responsible for ensuring the correct information is entered into the ESS System. 40.\")", 
"c(\"This includes the exercising of procedural powers in relation to directions, hearings, witnesses, evidence and submissions which are necessary to make the arbitration effective. 3.2.10 A party to the dispute may appoint another person to accompany or represent them in relation to the dispute. 3.2.11 The steps contained in paragraphs 3.2.4 to 3.2.6 are only required to be followed when the relevant employee is a union member. 3.3 INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE IN THE WORKPLACE 3.3.1 This clause applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Pick and pack orders, labeling boxes, participate in stock takes, must be competent to perform tasks including voice picking, pick by light, Carousel, Lidding, Despatch Sortation Systems, <strong>Automated</strong> Manual Handling equipment and operate basic equipment and RF unit.\", \"Assembly Pick and pack orders, labeling boxes, participate in stock takes, must be competent to perform tasks including voice picking, pick by light, Carousel, Lidding, Despatch Sortation Systems, <strong>Automated</strong> Manual Handling equipment and operate basic equipment and RF unit.\", \n\"Assembly Pick and pack orders, labeling boxes, participate in stock takes, must be competent to perform tasks including voice picking, pick by light, Carousel, Lidding, Despatch Sortation Systems, <strong>Automated</strong> Manual Handling equipment and operate basic equipment and RF unit.\", \"Assembly Pick and pack orders, labeling boxes, participate in stock takes, must be competent to perform tasks including voice picking, pick by light, Carousel, Lidding, Despatch Sortation Systems, <strong>Automated</strong> Manual Handling equipment and operate basic equipment and RF unit.\"\n)", 
"c(\"The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by the FWC in accordance with this term. 2.2 Consultation Regarding Major Workplace Change 1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"As part of Cement Australia's continuous improvement program, the Employer will introduce an <strong>automated</strong> and auditable time capture system for Employee drivers in Victoria.\")", 
"c(\"(ii) Skill Level E2 (C7 equivalent) Employees at this level have all the skills of Level El and have completed a minimum two certified courses in Siemens PLC and be competent in logic and program change as assessed by an external assessor or Electrical Team Leader (iii) Skill Level E3 (C6 equivalent) Employees at this level have all the skills of Level E2 and have completed a minimum two certified courses in Citect control logic and are deemed competent by an external assessor or Electrical Team Leader (iv) Skill Level E4 (C5 equivalent) Employees at this level have all the skills of Level E3 and are certified as competent in the <strong>automated</strong> operation of the Siemens Kmatic system, Kiln Process and associated field control panels as assessed by a site nominated assessor (d) Apprentices or Trainees (i) Apprentices or trainees engaged during the life of this Agreement will be covered by the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement.\", \n\"CONSULTATION (1) This term applies if the employer: (a)has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 8.1 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 8.1.1 1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Smoking on the site is prohibited. 10.4 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the worksite during work times, as this can effect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 10.5 Tool Issue All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\")", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken. 5.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery 5.9 Standing down of employees The employer shall have the right to deduct payment of any day the employee cannot be usefully employed because of any industrial action or through major breakdown(s) of machinery or any stoppage of work by any cause for which the employer cannot reasonably be held responsible.\", \n\"9.0 Consultation regarding major workplace changes (a) (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Any matter not covered in this Agreement shall be referred to the Consultative Committee established pursuant to Clause 7.1. 1.9 CONSULTATION (a) This term applies if the employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"arrangements; (ii) slaughterers shall remove skins and hides free from cuts and tears; 2.2.5 If there is a hold-up in work for any reason whatsoever, subject to Clauses 3.2 (Waiting Time) and 3.3 (Work On Time), then at the request of the Employer, the employees shall resume work in time to complete the tasks commenced and avoid any loss in production. 2.3 CHANGES TO SYSTEMS AND METHODS OF WORK 2.3.1 General decisions regarding methods of work, production processes, waste minimisation, introduction of machinery or new technology which may affect manning and tallies covered by this agreement, shall be made following consultation with the Consultative Committee established under Clause 7.1. 2.3.2 If the Employer contemplates an entirely new method of processing within the establishment, such as installation of machines or new technology, it will give the Union seven (7) days notice in writing in addition to consulting with the Consultative Committee pursuant to Clause 7.1. 2.3.3 (i) If <strong>automation</strong>\", \n\"or new technology is introduced and it is clearly evident that the <strong>automation</strong> or new technology has replaced a position/s and has no other effect in the process, the tally will be altered to reflect the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> or new technology.\", \"(ii) It is to be guaranteed that no employee will process any more units after the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> or new technology and the subsequent alteration to tally as a result of labour saving for the same amount of pay.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 3.7 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can effect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery.\", \"parties are trying to resolve the dispute using the procedures in this term: (a) an employee must continue to perform his or her work as he or she would normally unless he or she has a reasonable concern about an imminent risk to his or her health or safety; and (b) an employee must comply with a direction given by the employer to perform other available work at the same workplace, or at another workplace, unless: (i) the work is not safe; or (ii) applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or (iii) the work is not appropriate for the employee to perform; or (iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. (7) The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 9 Consultation 1)This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\")", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken. 5.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery 5.9 Standing down of employees The employer shall have the right to deduct payment of any day the employee cannot be usefully employed because of any industrial action or through major breakdown(s) of machinery or any stoppage of work by any cause for which the employer cannot reasonably be held responsible. o PAM 11\", \n\"9.0 Consultation regarding major workplace changes (a) (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 4.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 4.9 Tool Issue All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\", \"(i) the work is not safe; or (ii) applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or (iii) the work is not appropriate for the employee to perform; or (iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. 10 Consultation regarding Major Workplace Change (1) This term 1 applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 4.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 4.9 Tool Issue All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\", \"10 Consultation regarding Major Workplace Change (1) This term 1 applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Time Records a) Individual timesheets must be completed by the employee by way of an electronic log in/out system which is automatically submitted to the Production Coordinator for approval and then to nominated Personnel for processing. b) It is the responsibility of the Employee to log in at the start of their shift and log out at the end of their shift within the approved time period of no more than 10 minutes either side of the nominated start and finish times. c) Where an Employee fails to log and submit their daily timesheet via the <strong>automated</strong> system: i.\", \n\"This term applies if the Employer: a) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 19.2.\"\n)", 
"c(\"F <strong>Automation</strong> .\", \"O SEE <strong>Automation</strong> .\", \"O SEE <strong>Automation</strong> .\")",

(7) The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 9 Consultation This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Accordingly, the agreed salary sacrifice arrangement cannot result in a claim for underpayment of theAgreement rates of pay. 6.2 No Extra Claims No extra claims, shall be made during the period of this Agreement except for annual increases determined in accordance with clause 2.1. 6.3 Change Consultation (1) This clause applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Working of clinker stockpile within the clinker shed to ensure continuous supply to the Mill Loading of Limestone and gypsum into appropriate hoppers to ensure continuous supply to the Mill Understand the bulk loading system including providing assistance to transport operators using the <strong>automated</strong> weighbridge loading system and off-bridge loading.\")", 
"c(\"returned materials as required, including ensuring shelves are tidy; carrying out regular shelf checks; and periodically generates missing and lost item lists and checking for these items on shelf; Processesing new materials and repairing damaged materials; Maintaining correct Activ holdings information on gratisnet; Assisting in marketing of library resources, which includes monthly in- house display of new material and selection and preparation of resources for inclusion in Activ news; Assisting with journal accessioning and maintenance of the journal collection; and Skills, A qualification or working towards a qualification in Library and experience, Information Studies, or relevant library experience qualifications Basic reference enquiry fulfilment skills Effective interpersonal skills, including customer service skills written and verbal communication skills Independent work skills in a library environment, such as organisational / time management skills Computer literacy across <strong>automated</strong>\", \n\". - CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE Introduction of a Major Change 24.1 This term applies if the Employer: 24.2 Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>. 24.3 For the purposes of this term a major change is likely to have a significant effect on employees if it results in: 24.3.1 the termination of the employment of employees; 24.3.2 a major change in the composition, operation or size of the employer's workforce, or to the skills required of employees; or, 24.3.3 the elimination or diminution of job opportunities, including opportunities for promotion opportunity or tenure; or 24.3.4 the alteration of hours of work; or 24.3.5 the need to retrain employees or 24.3.6 the need to relocate employees to another job workplace; or 24.3.7 the restructuring of jobs. 24.4 The Employer must notify the relevant employees\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONTROL & ELECTRIC Building <strong>Automation</strong> | Mechanical Electrical | Switchboards | Service PTY LTD ABN: 04 135 853 780 www.controlandelectric.com.au CONTROL & ELECTRIC PTY LIMITED - SINGLE ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2016 11 Bayldon Road Queenbeyan NSW 2620 PO 8/x 787 Pyntwick ACT 2500 UNDERTAKING Telephone (02) 6299 6213 The following undertakings are made pursuant to section 190 of the Fair Fackmile (02: 8290 6744 Work Act 2009 in relation to the Control & Electric Pty Ltd - Single Enterprise Agreement 2016 (the Agreement). 1: Notwithstanding the rate shown in Schedule B of the Agreement, an Electrical Worker Grade 1 will be paid a basic periodic rate of pay of $19.25 per hour. 2.\", \n\"CONSULTATION TERM 13.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"CONTROL & ELECTRIC Solution Partner SIEMENS Building <strong>Automation</strong> | Mechanical Electrical | Switchboards | Service PTY LTD Technologie's ACN 135 853 780 ABN: 84 135 853 780 admin@controlandelectric.com.au www.controlandelectric.com.au CONTROL & ELECTRIC PTY LIMITED - SINGLE ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2016 11 Bayldon Road Queanbeyan NSW 2620 PO Box 787, Fyshwick ACT 2609 UNDERTAKING Telephone (02) 6299 6233 The following undertakings are made pursuant to section 190 of the Fair Facsimile (02) 6299 6244 Work Act 2009 in relation to the Control & Electric Pty Ltd - Single Enterprise ACT Lic 19802117 Agreement 2016 (the Agreement).\"\n)", 
"c(\"This vision is characterised by: > a lean operation with a high degree of <strong>automation</strong>, > highly skilled employees who are well remunerated, > a responsive business with fast changeovers and fast product development, a workforce who take responsibility for their own performance and the performance of their shift team and section, > a flexible workforce, with flexibility between function, other departments, and roster arrangements that cover five to seven day operations.\", \"Model Consultation term Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or VIP Packaging Laverton North EA 2019 Page 17 of 20\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation This applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 7.1 Major Change 7.1.1 For major change referred to in paragraph (a): 7.1.1.1 the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and 7.1.1.2 subclauses 7.1.2 and 7.1.8 apply. 7.1.2 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 7.1.3 lf: 7.1.3.1 a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and 7.1.3.2 the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; The employer must recognise the representative. 7.1.4 As soon as practicable after making\", \n\"Classification Rate <strong>Automated</strong> Packing Line Skill Payment: · Feed up, control, operate machine at rate, including process quality U requirements and record productivity information.\")",
