of the College community - Undertake the from time to time and competency of less senior transportation and distribution of inspections and assists in supplier evaluations. the definitions, - Is engaged\", \n\"Frequently interacting furniture to and from separate floor with members of the College levels or areas, the assembling and maintaining as required all buildings, grounds, plant, and distribution of customer service based on furniture to and from community and offering advice removing of flooring and seating equipment and <strong>automated</strong> systems. category may include: knowledge of College separate floor levels or and customer service based on systems for exams or catered - Is engaged to perform work across multiple trade operations. disciplines within their competencies.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This clause applies if the Company: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Operate computer systems and other <strong>automated</strong> equipment.\", \"Ability to operate relevant <strong>automated</strong> equipment and computer systems.\")", 
"c(\"(iii) how the employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of their employment as a result of the arrangement; and (e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. 2.2.1.4 The Company must give the employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 2.2.1.5 The Company or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement, or (b) if the Company and employee agree in writing - at any time. 2.2.1.6 The relevant employee(s) may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 2.3 Consultation 2.3.1 This term applies if the Company: 2.3.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 2.3.1.2 proposes to introduce a change\", \n\"Pot Operator Forklift Operator Material Handler (with forklift licence) Machine Operator Team Leader - Galvanising C11.1 Advanced Machine Operator Ladder Welder Pickler Senior Pot Operator Supervisor - Galvanising C11.2 Storeperson (with forklift licence) Truck Loader Channel Rollform <strong>Automated</strong> Machine Operator Fitting Welder C11.3 Jigs & Heavy Welder <strong>Automated</strong> Machine Programmer Pipe Support Operator Team Leader - Warehouse Team Leader - Production Supervisor - Production C11.4 Speciality Welder Team Leader - Fabrication Supervisor - Fabrication Supervisor - Warehouse C10 Trades Assistant C9 Fitter & Turner Toolmaker Boilermaker Electrician Other single trade Maintenance position C8 Fitter & Turner and Toolmaker Other dual trade Maintenance position C7 · Team Leader - Maintenance Korvest Ltd Kilburn Enterprise Agreement 2025 Page 41 of 44\"\n)", 
"c(\"If the Company has made a definite decision to introduce major workplace change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>, the Company must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change. b.\", \n\"Includes sachet operator and <strong>automated</strong> coin rolling machine.\")", 
"c(\"in writing another person to act on their behalf, which may be their Union and a senior executive of G4S Custodial Services Pty Ltd will become involved, and if necessary, the dispute may be referred to Fair Work Australia for conciliation and, if necessary, arbitration. 4.1.3 Depending upon the nature of the matter involved, considerations of urgency or the number of employees concerned, it may be appropriate for earlier stages of this dispute procedure to be expedited and the matter referred by either party directly to Fair Work Australia for conciliation and or arbitration. 4.1.4 It is agreed that the status quo, at the time of the dispute being notified, shall remain. 4.1.5 Nothing in the above dispute settling procedure shall operate to the prejudice of the workforce or the health and safety of any of the parties. 4.2 Consultation 4.2.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \"Provided that any employer shall not be required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be inimical to the employee's interests. 6.2.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 5.1.1 of this Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by G4S of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which G4S is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken. 5.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery 5.9 Standing down of employees The employer shall have the right to deduct payment of any day the employee cannot be usefully employed because of any industrial action or through major breakdown(s) of machinery or any stoppage of work by any cause for which the employer cannot reasonably be held responsible. 9 AAM\", \n\"Amendment commencing on 1 January 2014 Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 4.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can effect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 4.9 Tool Issue All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\", \"(iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. 10 Consultation regarding Major Workplace Change (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or - (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 34.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"TECHNOLOGICAL CHANGE It is agreed that all drivers shall co-operate with the introduction of: GPS Scheduling Automatic Weighing <strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection Other Technological Changes Management shall involve employees in discussions regarding the introduction of technological change.\")", 
"c(\"Working together on continuous improvement activities to reduce labour costs as a percentage of sales through efficient work practices and assisting in the implementation of <strong>automation</strong> of processes. vi.\", \"Consultation (1) This term applies if Viscount Plastics: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation regarding workplace change 12.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Spice room prebatching with full inventory and traceability Cycle counts and stock reconciliation Level 4 (92.4% An employee at Level 4 has: Competencies working Has met requirements of level 3 with competencies in relativity to the under general supervision one or more Level 3 Skill sets tradesperson) completed an AQF Certificate 2 in Food but requires higher level of Kitchen cooking and ingredient preparation, product | Processing; or Judgement transfers, and control of <strong>automated</strong> CIP (ii) has equivalent recognised enterprise or SAP receipt and transfers of materials, cycle counts and industrial experience, training or prior learning stock reconciliation and inventory control experience and/or skills to Level 4.\"\n)", 
"c(\"discussion-provide to the Relevant Employees: 13.3.4(b).1 all relevant information about the change, including the nature of the change; and 13.3.4(b).2 information about what AAP reasonably believes will be the effects of the change on the Relevant Employees; and 13.3.4(b).3 information about any other matters that AAP reasonably believes are likely to affect the Relevant Employees; and 13.3.4(c) invite the Relevant Employees to give their views about the impact of the change (including any impact in relation to their family or caring responsibilities). 13.3.4(d) However, AAP is not required to disclose confidential or commercially sensitive information to the Relevant Employees. 13.3.4(e) AAP must give prompt and genuine consideration to matters raised about the change by the Relevant Employees. 13.4.1 AAP recognises the changing media landscape and the different technology needs associated with Employees undertaking editorial work. 13.4.2 On that basis, where AAP makes a decision to implement <strong>Artificial</strong>\", \n\"<strong>Intelligence</strong> technology that would directly impact the editorial operations performed by Employees, AAP agrees to discuss this and any associated workplace changes with those Employees. 13.5 Consultative meetings with MEAA There shall be an annual consultation meeting held between AAP and MEAA representatives to promote ongoing workplace dialogue and to discuss workplace issues.\")", 
"c(\"Consultation 2.13.1 This clause applies if the Company: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Simple general chemistry tests or using basic <strong>automated</strong> equipment (e.g. pH, EC, Alkalinity, Colour, Turbidity, ISE Fluoride, TOC, BOD, O&G), gravimetric tests (e.g.\", \"GC-FID, PT- GCMS, HS-GCMS, ICP-OES) Microbiology - operation of <strong>automated</strong> instrument (e.g.\", \"An <strong>automated</strong> process will be run prior to the pay to provide a variance report identifying actual earnings in the pay cycle and a comparison to the relevant Award earnings.\")", 
"c(\"This vision is characterised by: > a lean operation with a high degree of <strong>automation</strong>, > highly skilled employees who are well remunerated, VIP Packaging Altona North EA 2023 Page 4 of 23\", \"Model Consultation term Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) (a) This term applies if the employer: has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees Major change (2) For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) major change; and the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the (b) (3) subclauses (3) to (9) apply.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation Regarding Major Workplace Change 13.1 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Time and Attendance 47.1 It is a requirement of employment that all staff must use our <strong>automated</strong> time and attendance system.\")", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 9.1 This term applies if the employer: 9.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 9.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"20.2.3 Workscope use of <strong>automated</strong> computer aids to assist in the handling and control of items of food and equipment and the recording of information as required; availability for deployment in any work area or work centre on a part or whole of shift basis. 20.2.4 To progress to Airline Catering Services Attendant Level 3, the employee must successfully apply for a vacant position at that level. 20.3 Airline Catering Services Attendant Level 3 20.3.1 An employee who is expected and required to perform the functions of a Level 1 and 2 Catering Services Attendant and to be proficient in all work requirements of those levels. 20.3.2 Skills and qualifications complete knowledge of airline equipment requirements, stowage and food presentations; complete and extensive knowledge of various specialist work areas (minimum of two). 20.3.3 Workscope Duties and requirements include, but are not limited to: monitoring techniques/efficiency/safe handling; .. modifying performance in accordance with Company\", \n\"making recommendations on all aspects of the operation, procedures; seeking to improve performance and productivity; ensuring the Catering and Quality Assurance procedure. regulations and safety standards are followed by staff; enforcing State established regulations and local standards pertaining to hygiene and safe food handling practices; demonstrating leadership, decision making and organised skills necessary to efficiently meet performance requirements in a changing work environment; making recommendations on all aspects of the operation, seeking to identify opportunities to improve performance and productivity; various administrative and reporting duties: skilled use of knives and machinery; implementation of directions given by Supervisory Chef and Management staff: providing raw material preparation and cooking assistance to Chefs; repetitive use of special purpose, pre-adjusted or <strong>automated</strong> cooking equipment not requiring the possession or use of trades skill or knowledge; training\", \n\"control checks of finished product ensuring food is to specification and correct presentation. 20.4 Leading Hand (Airline Catering Service Attendant): 20.4.1 A level 2 or 3 Airline Catering Services Attendant who is appointed as such and who may be required to: ensure that productivity and performance criteria are met in the designated area of responsibility (including fulfilling regular performance assessment reports of staff supervised); be responsible for the control, supervision and/or training of new and current staff and assessment of their performance; co-ordinate manpower allocation (including overtime and sickness cover) in consultation with the supervisor to achieve maximum productivity and efficiency. 20.4.2 The employee must be able to communicate clearly and issue instructions appropriately. 20.5 Chef 20.5.1 A tradesperson who must complete a six month probation period and undergo airline catering orientation in the fields of: portion control; . . menu specification; basic <strong>Automated</strong>\"\n)",

and equipment under more <strong>automated</strong> direction to less - Frequently interacting with - Undertakes operational/ maintenance planning, - Undertake the limited or broad supervision. senior grades. members of the College community including scheduling and estimating materials, and offering advice and customer oversees and carries out quality control and physical transportation - Interacting with members - Being able to verify the and distribution of of the College community competency of less senior service based on extensive inspections and assists in supplier evaluations. furniture to and from from time to time and grades.\", \n\"Frequently interacting knowledge of College operations. - Is engaged in operating, monitoring and separate floor levels or offering advice and with members of the College - Undertake the physical maintaining as required all buildings, grounds, plant, areas, the assembling customer service based on community and offering advice transportation and distribution of equipment and <strong>automated</strong> systems. and removing of knowledge of College and customer service based on furniture to and from separate floor flooring and seating - Is engaged to perform work across multiple trade operations. extensive knowledge of College levels or areas, the assembling and disciplines within their competencies.\"\n)", 
"c(\"The Employees All employees are obliged to: a) Comply with all company safety rules and regulations including the use of all protective equipment. b) To work productively within job requirements and allocated tasks and to participate in the process of continuous improvement in all aspects of the enterprise's operation which includes: . direct productivity increases . occupational health & safety quality of product / quality assurance reduction of waste, wasted material and wasted time housekeeping and cleanliness reduction in the current level of controllable operating costs, ie maintenance, power etc. 4.1.2 No Extra Claims Parties to this Agreement will not pursue and extra claims to wage rates or terms of employment over and beyond those provided in this Agreement during the prescribed life of this Agreement. 4.1.3 Consultation This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong>\", \n\"<strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 9.4 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can effect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 9.5 Tool Issue All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Page 7 | Bathurst Signs and Screen Printing Enterprise Agreement 2016 engraving, cleaning, polishing and finishing of any of such signs in paint, decorative or protective surface coatings or coverings (G) Assisting in the provision of on the job training in conjunction with other tradespersons, Non Tradespersons and Supervisors; (H) Operating a wide range of complex machines or equipment including large format digital printers, flat bed printers and other printing equipment, <strong>automated</strong> engravers vinyl cutting, engraving equipment and lettering; (1) Maintaining records; (J) Inspecting products and or materials for conformity with established operational guidelines; (K) Using hand trolleys or palate trucks and (where qualified) forklifts or cherry-pickers; (L) Cleaning of floors, outdoor and factory areas; (M) Blending paints, resins and tints; (N) Installing signs including (where accredited) complex installation including working at height and electrical connections; (0) Operating, setting\", \n\"The stand down period may be up to 6 weeks in duration. 28 Consultation 28.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"The Arrangement will also outline: which term or terms of the Agreement the effect of which are varied; how the effect of the term or terms are varied, and what the outcome is intended to be. 1.8.3 An Arrangement may be terminated: (a) by either the Employee or the Employer giving 28 days' written notice in accordance with the Act; or (b) by the written consent of the Employee (and where the Employee is less than 18 years old by their parent or guardian) and the Employer at any time. 1.9 CONSULTATION 1.9.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: i. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or ii. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Maintain <strong>automated</strong> production processes.\")", 
"c(\"under 18 years of age, signed by a parent or guardian of the Employee; and d) includes details of: i. the terms of this Agreement that will be varied by the arrangement; and = how the arrangement will vary the effect of the terms; and ili. how the Employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of his or her employment as a result of the arrangement; and iv. states the day on which the arrangement commences. 11.4 The College must give the Employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 11.5 The College or the Employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: a) by giving 28 days' written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or b) if the College and the Employee agree in writing - at any time. 12 Consultation Regarding Major Workplace Change 12.1 This term applies if: a) the College has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 12.2 As soon as practicable, the College must notify the relevant Employees and the Union of the decision to introduce the major change. 12.3 The Employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in\", \n\"Advise on the use and selection of search engines, <strong>automated</strong> information systems or databases.\", \"77 | Page Duties may include: Library - 4 Carry out an extensive range of library duties, showing a high degree of specialised knowledge including; o Advanced use of library systems o Liaising extensively with other senior library staff and teachers in relation to programs, resources and facilities 0 Advanced use of systems for retrieval of resources Managing the <strong>automated</strong> library system o Original cataloguing and classification Collection selection and development O Organising the stocktake o Undertaking specific projects O Taking responsibility for AV and/or ICT resources, including editing O Providing copyright advice Assisting with Budget preparation and research 0 0 Supervising volunteers (parents, students) Assisting with the evaluation and selection equipment and make recommendations for purchase 0 o Preparing purchase orders for equipment and materials.\", \n\"Make significant contributions to the development of technical and operational procedures. 0 Train staff and students in use of the <strong>automated</strong> library system. o Advanced use of SCIS for original cataloguing and classification of resources. o Maintain the resource centre intranet/web pages (adding, modifying pages). 0 Assist teaching staff with collection development (e.g. suggestions for new resources).\")", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 3.9 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery.\", \"the parties are trying to resolve the dispute using the procedures in this term: (a)an employee must continue to perform his or her work as he or she would normally unless he or she has a reasonable concern about an imminent risk to his or her health or safety; and (b)an employee must comply with a direction given by the employer to perform other available work at the same workplace, or at another workplace, unless: (i) the work is not safe; or (ii) applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or (iii) the work is not appropriate for the employee to perform; or (iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. (7) The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 9 Consultation This term applies if the employer; (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 6.004129960 Draft employee collective agreement page 14\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the Company: 1.7.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong> (\\\"Major Change\\\"); or 1.7.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees (\\\"Roster Change\\\"). 1.7.2.\", \n\"Has achieved a score of at least 6450 points on the Electrical Competency Points Register Performs operations requiring basic skills to fault find complex PLC , VSD , safety control systems and <strong>automation</strong> processes Repairs and maintains, tests, modifies or fault finds on machinery which utilises but not limited to mechanical, hydraulic and/or pneumatic principles and in the course of such work, is required to read and understand electronic, hydraulic and pneumatic circuitry drawings, instruction or manuals which controls <strong>automation</strong> systems Enterprise Agreement - Maintenance 2023- Final\", \n\"Installs, repairs and maintains, tests, modifies, commissions and/or fault finds on complex machinery which utilises <strong>automation</strong> of hydraulic and/or pneumatic principles and in the course of such work, is required to read and understand hydraulic and pneumatic circuitry which controls fluid power systems above that is required of level D Works on complex or intricate circuitry which involves examining, diagnosing and modifying systems comprising of interconnected circuits Mechanical Stream .\", \n\"Performs work above and beyond the skills of an employee at level E and: a) Install, maintain, and replace <strong>automation</strong> systems at key points in the production process b) Periodically report to leadership regarding <strong>automation</strong> capabilities and status c) Capable of being an effective project team leader engaging all relevant stakeholders Tasks of which an employee at this level may perform are the following: Common to both Trades (Mechanical and Electrical Streams) .\", \n\"Ensures proper operation of SCADA system, identifies and troubleshoots issues pertaining to SCADA system Assists project team to integrate new machines to existing <strong>automation</strong> networks .. lead <strong>automation</strong> projects and <strong>automation</strong> upgrades Enterprise Agreement - Maintenance 2023- Final\")", 
"c(\"Lead MAC Load Main <strong>Automation</strong> Control Mandatory Training Training required to comply with legislative obligations, safety case requirements and Shell Group global beravioural and compliance standards, as may change from time to time. 2\", \"Lead MAC Lead Main <strong>Automation</strong> Control.\", \"This clause applies if the Company: 11.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or 11.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 36.1 This term applies if the employer: a) is considering to make a decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 36.2 Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (36.1) (a) and prior to implementing the decision and major change: a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the proposed decision to introduce the major change; and b) subclauses (36.3) to (36.9) apply. 36.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 36.4 If: 21\", \n\"TECHNOLOGICAL CHANGE It is agreed that all drivers shall co-operate with the introduction of: GPS Scheduling · GPS Automatic Weighing <strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection Electronic Work Diaries (EWD) Camera units that capture in cab, forward and side views Other Technological Changes Management shall involve employees in discussions regarding the introduction of technological change.\")", 
"c(\"(a) GPS Scheduling; (b) Automatic Weighing; (c) <strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection; and (d) Other Technological Changes; 40.2 The management of SCT Logistics will involve Drivers in discussions regarding the introduction of technological change.\", \"47 CONSULTATION 47.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 47.2 Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (47.1)(a): (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (47.3) to (47.9) apply. 47.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 47.4 If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise the representative. 47.5 As soon as practicable after making its\"\n)",

is engaged in operating, monitoring and maintaining as required all buildings, grounds, plant, equipment and <strong>automated</strong> systems; is engaged to perform work across multiple trade disciplines within their competencies.\"\n)", 
"c(\"(e) A Casual Employee's right to request conversion to full or part time employment is as per the NES. 5.5 Employee Duties At all times, You must: (a) Devote the whole of your time, attention, and skill during your hours of work to the performance and discharge of your duties; (b) Carry out your duties to the standard Stramit requires; (c) Act honestly, faithfully, and diligently in the performance of your duties; (d) Conform to and comply with Stramit's lawful and reasonable directions; (e) Present for work in appropriate attire; (f) Comply with all Stramit policies and procedures and any other standards developed and communicated to you from time to time; (g) Undertake work-related training as directed by Stramit and comply with all new practices and procedures; (h) Cooperate with changes in work procedures and/or equipment <strong>automation</strong> to improve quality and/or operation; Ensure to the best of your ability that equipment standards and product quality is maintained; Provide Stramit with\", \n\"Consultation and Major Change 26.1 General (a) This term applies if Stramit: (i) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees. 26.2 Obligations regarding consultation (a) For a major change referred to in paragraph 26.1(a)(i) (i) Stramit must notify the relevant Employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and .. . - (ii) Sub-clauses 26.2(c) to (h) apply.\"\n)", 
"c(\"313 Security Services means 313 Business Group PTY LTD trading as 313 Security Services 1.7 Period of Operation 1.7.1 This Agreement will commence, in accordance with the Act, once it has been approved by Fair Work Australia. 1.7.2 This Agreement will nominally expire on 31st December 2017. 1.7.3 This Agreement shall remain valid for all matters that may arise after it's expiry which related to events or references that fall with-in the period of its operation. 1.8 Consultation 313 Security seeks to adopt the model consultation term. 1.8.1 This term applies if the employer: 1.8.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 1.8.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 1.8.2 For a major change referred to in paragraph (1.8.1.1): 1.8.2.1 the employer must notify the\", \n\"Provided that 313 Security shall not be required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which, when looked at objectively, would be inimical to the interests of 313 Security. 5.2.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 5.1.1 of this Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by 313 Security of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which 313 Security Is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination.\"\n)",

and (e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. 3.4 Murray Goulburn will give the Employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 3.5 Murray Goulburn or Employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement (or such period as is prescribed by the FW Act); and (b) subclauses 4.3 to 4.7 apply. 4.3 As soon as practicable after making its decision, Murray Goulburn must: (a) discuss with the relevant Employees and the NUW (or any other nominated representative): (i) the introduction of the change; "c(\"In support of maintaining a multi skilled workforce, <strong>automated</strong> machinery and accommodating cyclic loadings within the factory, it is accepted that employees will be required to work in a section other than their normal area of work. 3.6 With the achievement of the above items, the parties will enhance customer relationships and thus promote The Company's ability to win additional business aimed at ensuring longevity of employment and a profitable business. 4.\", \"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE Model consultation term 32.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees Major change 32.2 For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (3) to (9) apply. 32.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 32.4 If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and Sandvik Mining and Construction Australia (Production Supply) Pty Ltd Bayswater Enterprise Agreement 2016 Page 21 of 31\"\n)",

"c(\"The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by the FWC in accordance with this term. 2.2 1 IS on Regar g Major workplace ige 1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"As part of Cement Australia's continuous improvement program, the Employer will introduce an <strong>automated</strong> and auditable time capture system for Employee drivers in Victoria.\")", 
"c(\"Such duties include, but are not limited to: Cleaning; housekeeping; picking; packing; replenishing; loading and unloading of vehicles; operation of computer equipment; operation of licensed or non-licensed materials handling equipment, including Fork Lift Trucks; unloading and despatch of goods; operation of <strong>automated</strong> storage and retrieval systems; operation of Radio Frequency (RF) equipment; checking; despatch and receiving administration; on job training and assessment of Associates in Training, and other duties as directed. 9\", \n\"Appendix A: Schedule 2.3-Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)",

"(refer clause 14.1 ) 15.10 It is the company's intention that no technician should work in excess of 20 weekends per year, however in certain circumstances there may be a requirement to do so. 16 <strong>AUTOMATED</strong> WORK SCHEDULING/GPS 16.1 In the event that employees are directed by the Company to the <strong>Automated</strong> Work Scheduling/GPS Device (or similar device) in the course of their employment.", 
"c(\"reasonable manner. 4.5.2 The Employees All employees are obliged to: a) Comply with all company safety rules and regulations including the use of all protective equipment. b) To work productively within job requirements and allocated tasks and to participate in the process of continuous improvement in all aspects of the enterprise's operation which includes: direct productivity increases occupational health & safety quality of product quality assurance reduction of waste, wasted material and wasted time housekeeping and cleanliness reduction in the current level of controllable operating costs, ie maintenance, power etc. 4.6 No Extra Claims Parties to this Agreement will not pursue and extra claims to wage rates or terms of employment over and beyond those provided in this Agreement during the prescribed life of this Agreement. 4.7 Consultation (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 10.2 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery. 10.3 Provision of Tools All employees will be issued with the appropriate tools to carry out the work that they have been employed to carry out.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE This term applies if the Employer: 13.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 13.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 7\", \n\"OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY The Company reserves the right to introduce new technology, systems, processes and programs including but not limited to robotics/<strong>automation</strong>, new machinery, new equipment, warehousing software or computer systems which can contribute to increasing the operational productivity, efficiency and/or effectiveness of the business.\")", 
"PART 4: WAGES AND RELATED MATTERS 4.1 Classification Structure 4.1.1 An employee shall commence as a Trainee and progress on the demonstration of competency at each level. 4.1.2 Trainee to Grade 1.6 shall be comprised of the following skill areas: Filling, labelling and can store; Order picking; Collect order centre and machine tinting; Unloading trucks and FS/RM replenishment; Loading trucks; Paint making and pot wash thinner manufacturers 4.1.3 Grade 1.7 shall be comprised of the following skill areas: Operation of robotic palletiser & depallatiser Operation of automatic filling stations Operation of <strong>automated</strong> paint transfer system * Operation of <strong>automated</strong> stretch wrapping system Operation of automatic denester Operation of automatic labellers 4.1.4 Grade 2 shall be comprised of the following skill areas: Colour matching and testing; Coordination of warehouse despatch and claims; Dispersion. 4.1.5 Grade 2.1 shall be comprised of the following skill areas: Raw materials - pigment preparation", 
"c(\"Biometric Timekeeping is an <strong>automated</strong> timekeeping method to recognize an employee based upon a physiological or behavioral characteristic.\", \"Employee is under 18 years of age, signed by a parent or guardian of the Employee; and d) includes details of: vi. the terms of the Agreement that will be varied by the arrangement; and vii. how the arrangement will vary the effect of the terms; and viii. how the Employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of his or her employment as a result of the arrangement; and e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. 56.4 The Company must give the Employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 56.5 The Company or Employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: a) by giving no more than twenty-eight (28) days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or b) if the Company and employee agree in writing - at any time. 57 CONSULTATION TERM 57.1 This term applies if the Company: a) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 28 | Page\")", 
"Classification Hourly Rates Permanent Casual Supervisor Level 1 $50.78 $60.93 Supervisor Level 2 $49.01 $58.81 Team Leader Level 1 $44.71 $53.65 Team Leader Level 2 $42.36 $50.83 Tradesperson Level 1 $40.57 $48.68 Tradesperson Level 2 $35.60 $42.72 Tradesperson Level 3 $30.28 $36.34 Tradesperson Level 4 $26.86 $32.23 <strong>Automation</strong> Level 1 $33.22 $39.86 <strong>Automation</strong> Level 2 $26.86 $32.23 <strong>Automation</strong> Level 3 $22.88 $27.46 Technical Assistant $29.25 $35.10 Trades Assistant Level 1 $26.86 $32.23 Trades Assistant Level 2 $22.88 $27.46 Trades Assistant Level 3 $19.76 $23.71 7.2 An apprentice employee (or employees under the age of 21) will be paid no less than the appropriate hourly rate of pay indicated in the table below: Apprentice/Employee by Age Hourly Rate Age 16 years $10.19 Age 17 years $14.55 Age 18 years $16.79 Age 19 years $19.76 Age 20 years $21.61 Adult Apprentices $26.86 7.3 The above minimum rates of pay for employees engaged under Clause 7.2 will be reviewed in line with any changes", 
"Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.", 
"Nothing in the foregoing shall infringe upon the right of the Company to make lawful decisions i.e. the right to manage. 129 Where a vessel is required to call at ports where <strong>automated</strong> mooring facilities are not available, it is agreed that the manning levels applied as a result of the introduction of <strong>automated</strong> mooring arrangement will represent the agreed minimum manning level of Chief Integrated Rating, 3 x Integrated Ratings, Chief Cook and Chief Caterer for operations in those ports. 1210 Where a vessel is in dry dock, and a crew member has to be repatriated, a replacement would not be sent to the vessel until immediately prior to departure from the port.", 
"c(\"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Meat Company Employees at this level perform tasks as contained in the definitions for the following roles: Printerm/machine operators Linker Operator (Sausage Room) Forklift Driver Standards officer (non-boning room) <strong>Automated</strong> Tray Labellers 3.4.5.\", \"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"The following vehicles, plant and equipment are to be used/operated: One person fully <strong>automated</strong> refuse collection vehicles.\", \"One person fully <strong>automated</strong> recycling collection vehicles.\")", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 42.1 This term applies if the employer: a) is considering to make a decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 42.2 Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (42.1) (a) and prior to implementing the decision and major change: a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the proposed decision to introduce the major change; and b) subclauses (42.3) to (42.9) apply. 42.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 42.4 If: a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; 27\", \n\"SCT Logistics Parkes Enterprise Agreement 2023 · Automatic Weighing · <strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection · Other Technological Changes 46.2 Management shall involve employees in discussions regarding the introduction of technological change.\")", 
"c(\"INTRODUCTION TO CHANGE 10.1 This term applies if Hazeldenes: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Demonstrate a moderate amount of operational <strong>automated</strong> and pre-determined product Stock Spin Chill discretion and judgment Keeping Units (SKU's) E Bagging & Target Batcher Works in a team environment Use of high pressure hoses to clean machinery within Value Add . .\")", 
"c(\"holds a current vehicle licence for heavy rigid level and operates: High Pressure Water Jet Unit 75 - 150 horsepower Operates 5 inch vacuum units Operates Gully Sucker/Eductor Catalyst change outs Drain cleaning equipment and site duties Tank cleaning operations Confined space work Operates access and lifting equipment Executes a range of manual cleaning and labouring duties as required All appropriate tasks are to be carried out under direction of Operator Grade 2 or above A Grade 1 Operator shall receive training in the operation of equipment applicable to Operator Grade 2 and above Completion of at least 912 hours work experience as Operator Grade 1 is required before an employee can be promoted to Operator Grade 2 Operator Grade 2 An employee who is engaged as such, holds a current vehicle licence for heavy rigid level: Operates High Pressure Water Jet 150 - 220 horsepower Operates 6 inch vacuum unit Operates Gully Sucker/Eductor Assist in the set up of specialised equipment such as <strong>automated</strong>\", \n\"items of equipment for both Industrial Services and Drain Cleaning equipment Plans and oversees confined space entry Performs scheduled maintenance duties at the appropriate authorisation level Liaise with Customer Service representative Ability to lead site activities during temporary absence of Team Leader Responsible for writing job safety analysis Perform risk analysis using Transpacific Guidelines An Operator Grade 3 shall receive training applicable to Team Leader responsibilities Must complete at least 1,976 hours work as Operator Grade 3 before they can be considered for promotion to Team Leader position Receive a satisfactory appraisal of work performance from the relevant Supervisor(s) Team Leader An employee who is engaged as such and operates High Pressure Water Jet 300 - 470 horsepower Operates 8 inch vacuum unit Operates other major items of equipment for both Industrial Services and Drain Cleaning equipment Set up and lead the operation of specialised equipment such as <strong>automated</strong>\", \n\"24.4 If the matter in dispute cannot be resolved at the workplace level, either party may refer the matter to the Fair Work Commission. 24.5 The Fair Work Commission will first attempt to resolve the dispute as it considers appropriate, including by mediation, conciliation, expressing an opinion or making a recommendation; and if the Fair Work Commission is unable to resolve the dispute at the first stage, the Fair Work Commission may then arbitrate the dispute; and make a determination that is binding on the parties. 24.6 Where the matter in dispute remains unresolved, the Fair Work Commission may exercise any method of dispute resolution permitted by the Act that it considers appropriate to ensure the settlement of the dispute. 25 Consultation 25.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>\"\n)",

be. 1.10.3 An Arrangement may be terminated: (a) by either the Employee or the Employer giving 28 days' written notice in accordance with the Act; or (b) by the written consent of the Employee (and where the Employee is less than 18 years old by their parent or guardian) and the Employer at any time. 1.11 CONSULTATION 1.11.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a
