part and tool design, and robotics, to progress to SK8 X X Undertake and complete a Diploma in Management or other relevant leadership course to progress to SK8 X X Active involvement in collecting, monitoring and analysing data in relation to KPI's (key performance indicators) relevant to the Die setting function x X Be involved and take the lead where required in continuous improvement projects within the moulding area X X Makes recommendations to improve procedures - continuous improvement X X By successful application for vacancy (dependent on the numbers of Die Setters required) X Be actively involved with tooling projects from conception through to commissioning stage X Assist in design of both part and tooling for new projects X Initiate and/or undertake major projects within the mould shop for continuous improvement, such including shop floor setup or <strong>automated</strong>\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: a. is considering to make a decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 41.2.\", \n\"<strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection d.\")", 
"c(\"JALNA DAIRY FOODS PTY LTD ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2019 o line changeovers (lids, film, buckets, SRTs, pouches etc.) o arrange immediate work groups - work and meal break rotations induct and instruct new staff MACHINE OPERATOR Underpinning modern award: Food, Beverage and Tobacco Manufacturing Award 2010 Underpinning modern award classification: Level 2 or 3 Description In addition to the basic duties and responsibilities of a New Starter, an Employee in this classification: · is primarily responsible for products flowing through the complete yoghourt filling line, including the set-up, smooth operation and clean-up of all associated machines constantly monitors the operation of the complete yoghourt filling line and <strong>automation</strong> systems to minimise downtime, anticipate and solve problems and identify improvement opportunities whilst ensuring safe, in spec quality products are produced follows Management, Supervisor, Leading Hand instructions ... is able to work autonomously or as part of a\", \n\"team to complete the task at hand could be given the following duties: o operate and monitor the entire yoghourt filling lines o transfer products from vats to filler o inspect and monitor product o operate and monitor yoghourt filling line <strong>automation</strong> system and C.I.P set 0 conduct and record quality control activities and checks 0 line changeovers arrange immediate work groups - work and meal break rotations 0 date code verification 0 factory cleaning and sanitation participate in continuous improvement initiatives that increase machine efficiency and/or improve the work area observe and raise awareness to any input material shortages to ensure plant efficiencies are met 0 collect samples and apply simple identification techniques 0 measure basic quantity levels induct and instruct new staff PASTEURISER / BATCH ROOM OPERATOR Underpinning modern award: Food, Beverage and Tobacco Manufacturing Award 2010 Page 50 of 54\", \n\"JALNA DAIRY FOODS PTY LTD ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2019 Underpinning modern award classification: Level 3 or 4 Description In addition to the basic duties and responsibilities of a New Starter, an Employee in this classification: is primarily responsible for operating pasteurisers, homogenisers, separator, batch tanks, silos, C.I.P equipment, <strong>automation</strong> and monitoring systems whilst ensuring safe and quality products are produced constantly monitors each filling lines' performance to minimise downtime, anticipate and sole problems and identify improvement opportunities whilst ensuring safe and quality products are produced follows Management instructions and daily production schedule . is able to work autonomously or as part of a team to complete the task at hand . could be given the following duties: . o operate and monitor all equipment related to the pasteuriser and batch room 0 process and bulk fill / dispatch milk and cream o batch up the yoghourt mix transfer product to / from batch tanks\", \n\", vats and silos 0 0 operate and monitor pasteuriser and batch room <strong>automation</strong> system and C.I.P set .\")", 
"c(\"Introduction of Change (1) This term applies if the company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees, Major change (2) For a major change referred to in paragraph (1)(a): (a) The company must notify the relevant employees and their union of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (3) to (9) apply. (3) The relevant employees may be represented by their union or appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. (4) If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the company of the identity of the representative; the company must recognise the representative. (5)\", \n\"22.4 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 22.4:1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy that employee must be given notice of termination as set out in sub-clause 20.1, or otherwise paid in lieu, as set out in sub-clause 20.2. 22.4.2 If the company makes payment instead of notice for all or any of the period of notice, then the period for which the payment is made must be treated as service for the purposes of calculating any service related entitlements of the employee arising in accordance with this enterprise agreement and is considered to be service with the company for the purposes of the Long Service Leave Act and the accrual of annual leave. 22.5 Period of notice of termination on redundancy because of <strong>automation</strong> or technological change 22.5.1 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in relation\", \n\"to which the company is engaged must be given not less than three months specific notice of termination. 22.5.2 <strong>Automation</strong> is defined as when new machinery does not require the use of labour.\", \"<strong>Automation</strong> and technological change, for example, is not the installation of a new bottling line or a new model of equipment.\", \"FOR EXAMPLE THE COMPANY WANTS TO INTRODUCE <strong>AUTOMATION</strong>, SUCH AS ROBOTICS, TO A PARTICULAR DEPARTMENT.\")", 
"c(\"The Arrangement will also outline: . which term or terms of the Agreement the effect of which are varied; . how the effect of the term or terms are varied, and . what the outcome is intended to be. 1.8.3 An Arrangement may be terminated: (a) by either the Employee or the Employer giving 28 days' written notice in accordance with the Act; or (b) by the written consent of the Employee (and where the Employee is less than 18 years old by their parent or guardian) and the Employer at any time. 1.9 CONSULTATION 1.9.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or Ordinary Hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Maintain <strong>automated</strong> production processes.\")", 
"NULL", "c(\"Hpour; (b) because the job has become <strong>automated</strong> and the Company no longe roquies it to be performed by an employee; or ) because of\\\" the insolvency or bankruptcy of the Company. (2) Amount of redundancy pay An employee whose employment 's torminatod by reason of redundancy (i.t. retanched; wil be paid troc (8) weexa for each completou your pt continuous period of soiv'os, up to a maximum of sixty (60) weers.\", \"evidence of attendance at an interview of the employee or they will not receive payment for the time absent. 9.2 Redundancy, redeployment and retrenchment (1) Redundancy pay An employee is entitled to be paid redundancy pay by the Company if the employee's employment is terminated: (a) at the Company's initiative because the Company no longer required the job to be done by the employee, to be done by anyone, except where this is due to the ordinary and customary turnover of labour; because the job has become <strong>automated</strong> and the Company no longer requires it to be performed by an employee; or because of the insolvency or bankruptcy of the Company.\", \n\"eighteen (18) years of age, signed by a parert or guardian of the employee; and (d) includes details of: the terms of the Agreement that will be varied by the arrangement; and (ii) how the arrangement will vary the effect of the terms; and (iii) how the employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of his or her employment as a result of the arrangement; and (e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. (4) The Company must give the employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within fourteen (14) days after it is agreed to. (5) The Company or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a by giving no more than twenty-eight (28) days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or (b) if the Company and employee agree in writing, at any time. 7.2 Consultation (1) This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\")", 
"c(\"Potential <strong>automation</strong> of the recording process will be investigated within the first six months after certification of this agreement. 25.4 Procedure 25.4.1 Hydrolyte powder will be provided when the average inside temperature reaches 30 degrees Celsius. 25.4.2 When the average inside temperature reaches 35 degrees Celsius, chilled spring water will be issued one per employee per shift period at no charge, during their first heat relief break. 25.4.3.\", \"CONSULTATION IN THE WORKPLACE 34.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"28.2 Consultation Term 28.2.1 (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"the employees to become redundant a selection criteria shall be developed as mutually agreed. 28.5 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 28.5.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by sub-clause 8.5.1 of the Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination. 28.5.2 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in sub-clause 8.5.1. herein the employer shall pay to that employee an amount calculated in accordance with the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the Employer: 10.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 10.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Production SP 10 Production SP 10 Production SP7 10 Production Cartridge 10 Production SPOB 10 Production SPPouch 10 Production <strong>Automated</strong> Pigging 10 Production BC Filling Line 10 Production Pallet Stretch Wrap Station 5 19.7.\")", 
"c(\"FILLING, CAPPING, SEALING, LABELLING AND PACKING MACHINES To be trained in the setting, adjustment and the operating conditions of an operation system(<strong>Automated</strong>/Manual) TRAINING NA QUALITY .\", \"FILLING, CAPPING, SEALING, LABELLING AND PACKING MACHINES To be fully competent and assessed in the setting, adjustment and the operating conditions of an operation system(<strong>Automated</strong>/Manual) TRAINING .\", \"To be fully competent and assessed in the operation of all bleach batching systems on site - <strong>automated</strong> or manual .\", \n\"To be fully competent and assessed in the operation of all bleach batching systems in plant - <strong>automated</strong> or manual .\", \"FILLING, CAPPING, SEALING, LABELLING AND PACKING MACHINES To be fully competent and assessed in the setting, adjustment, and the operating . conditions of an operation system(<strong>Automated</strong>/Manual) .\")", 
"c(\"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"notify the employer of the employee's intention to resume work after an absence, no later than 2:00 PM on the working day before the day of intended resumption of work; and 19.5.4 Should any employee attend an offer for work after any absence without first advising the employer in accordance with the requirements of clause 19.5.3 the employer shall not be obliged to employ such employee on that particular day and the employee shall not be entitled to payment for that day if they are not employed. 19.6 Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as preventing the employment of any person partly on work in respect of which provision is made by this Agreement and partly on other work, and in such case this Agreement shall apply to the employment of such employee if the major and substantial part of the employee's work is work in respect of which provision is made by this Agreement. 19.7 Use of Time Recording Systems / Time Clocks Where an employee is required to clock on and off using an <strong>automated</strong>\"\n)", 
"c(\"Potential <strong>automation</strong> of the recording process will be investigated within the first six months after certification of this agreement. 25.4 Procedure 25.4.1 Chilled diet (low sugar) cordial will be provided when the average inside temperature reaches 30 degrees Celsius 25.4.2 When the average inside temperature reaches 35 degrees Celsius, chilled spring water will be issued one per employee per shift period at no charge, during their first heat relief break. 25.4.3.\", \"CONSULTATION IN THE WORKPLACE 34.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Operates at and often exceeds machine and bullnose / profile cutting, using advanced cutting the required productivity and quality standards over time, plays a machines (Baumer), looper operation, small peeler operation, looper key role in housekeeping in the work area, is highly active in set up and troubleshooting, bun handling and storage, Dolphin Pack safety initiatives (e.g. risk assessments, hazard identification, operation, operation of bun management <strong>automation</strong> (Travellator, incident investigation) and makes suggestions for the development ABLG1, ABLG2, BZMA), supervision of chemical unloading, of job specific systems and for the overall improvement of the understanding of foam making chemicals, production line operation, work area. production line setup, operation of control panels, raw materials and stock control, participating in multi skilling program, data recording for production planning raw materials to be used and stock control ,.\", \n\"APPENDIX C MODEL CONSULTATION TERM (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION (a) This term applies if the Company: (i). has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (ii). proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Is able to communicate clearly and follow up in providing information Works cooperatively with internal and external customers Provides team members with feedback as to performance and behaviour Safety Follows safe work practices and can report workplace hazards Develop and implement SWMS Role models safe work practices Ensures team adheres to safe work practices Proactively identifies and identifies workplace hazards Behaviours After adequate instruction, the employee may be required to undertake any task(s) listed below An employee at this level would normally perform the following indicative tasks (CORE) Supervision (own and of others) C Responsibility C Discretion C Degree of Autonomy C Quality C Communication, Customer Service & Relations C Safety C Skills/Tasks Plant Maintenance proficiency (ability to work, troubleshoot, & resolve alone) C PLC competency C Basic <strong>Automation</strong> C Intermediate Microsoft Office 0 SAP (Maintenance Module) C Tradesperson (Electrical/Electronic/Mechanical/\"\n)", 
"c(\"(iii) For these reasons the Employees have agreed to actively work in cooperation with Management of The Austral Brick Company Pty Ltd to achieve its goal of increasing the productivity of Plant personnel to current world's best practice by taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video and telecommunications as well as computerisation).\", \"Austral Bricks NSW Production Workers Enterprise Agreement 2018 Sept 2018 (i) the work is not safe; or (ii) applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or (iii) the work is not appropriate for the employee to perform; or (iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. (7) The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. (8) Any decision or other binding outcome of the Fair Work Commission must be consistent with the Code for the Tendering and Performance of Building Work 2016. 8.3 CONSULTATION Consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster\"\n)", 
"NULL", "NULL", "c(\"Skill Payment Table 15.3 2.00% 2.00% 2.75% Skill category Current rate (weekly) Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 Senior Operator Skills: Q $25.49 $26.00 $26.52 $27.25 R $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 Machine Operator Skills (Slitter Operation): M $12.11 $12.36 $12.60 $12.95 M Basic $6.18 $6.30 $6.43 $6.61 P $25.49 $26.00 $26.52 $27.25 L $25.49 $26.00 $26.52 $27.25 K $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 N $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 Machine Operator Skills (Shearline Operation): F $12.11 $12.36 $12.60 $12.95 F Basic $6.18 $6.30 $6.43 $6.61 J $25.49 $26.00 $26.52 $27.25 G $25.49 $26.00 $26.52 $27.25 H $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 E $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 Recoiler: T $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 <strong>Automated</strong> Packing Line: U $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 Store/Machine Assistant: D $18.29 $18.66 $19.03 $19.55 16.\", \n\"Classification Rate <strong>Automated</strong> Packing Line Skill Payment: · Feed up, control, operate machine at rate, including process quality U requirements and record productivity information.\")", 
"c(\"Redundancy 28.1 Notification of Intended Changes 28.1.1 Where the employer has made a definite decision to implement changes in production, programming, organisation, structure or technology that are likely to have significant effects on employees, the employer shall as soon as practicable notify the employees who may be affected by the proposed changes and the Joint Consultative Committee. 28.1.2 \\\"Significant Effects\\\" include termination of employment; major changes in the composition, operation or size of the employer's workforce or in the skills required; the elimination or diminution of job opportunities, promotion opportunities or job tenure; the alteration of hours of work; the need for retraining or transfer of employees to other work or locations and the restructuring of jobs. 28.2 Consultation Term 28.2.1 Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \"Provided that the employer shall not be required to disclose confidential information or any information that may seriously harm the employer's business undertakings or the employers interest in the carrying on, or disposition, of the business undertaking. 28.4.3 If terminations are unavoidable the employer shall call for volunteers to take redundancy packages, but if this does not produce the necessary reduction in labour determination of the employees to become redundant a selection criteria shall be developed as mutually agreed. 28.5 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 28.5.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by sub-clause 8.5.1 of the Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like KAT MAIN SEA 3 January 2023\"\n)", 
"c(\"(d) A Casual Employee's right to request conversion to full or part time employment is as per the Award 5.5 Employee Duties At all times, You must: (a) Devote the whole of your time, attention, and skill during your hours of work to the performance and discharge of your duties; (b) Carry out your duties to the standard Stramit requires; (c) Act honestly, faithfully, and diligently in the performance of your duties; (d) Conform to and comply with Stramit's lawful and reasonable directions; (e) Present for work in appropriate attire; (f) Comply with all Stramit policies and procedures and any other standards developed and communicated to you from time to time; (g) Undertake work-related training as directed by Stramit and comply with all new practices and procedures; (h) Cooperate with changes in work procedures and/or equipment <strong>automation</strong> to improve quality and/or operation; (1) Ensure to the best of your ability that equipment standards and product quality is maintained; () Provide Stramit\", \n\"Consultation and Major Change 26.1 General (a) This term applies if Stramit: (i) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees 26.2 Obligations regarding consultation (a) For a major change referred to in paragraph 26.1(a)(i) (i) Stramit must notify the relevant Employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (ii) Sub-clauses 26.2(c) to (h) apply.\"\n)", 
"c(\"INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 36.1 This term applies if: (a) the employer has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) the employer proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employess Major change 36.2 For a major change referred to in clause 36.1(a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change, and clauses 36.3 to 36.8 apply. 36.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 36.4 If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise the representative 36.5 As soon as practicable after making its\", \n\". - (Port Phillip Prison) Correctional Services Workplace Agreement 2017 Agreement provided that Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by G4S of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which G4S is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination.\")", 
"c(\"Other fully <strong>automated</strong> processes as they come on line.\", \". * Other fully <strong>automated</strong> processes as they come on line.\")", 
"Where a customer arrives when employee is locking up and does not have an <strong>automated</strong> card activated service, then the operator may choose to serve them and claim additional wages in fifteen minute increments.", 
"c(\"(d) age, signed by a parent or guardian of the employee; and includes details of: (i) the terms of the enterprise agreement that will be varied by the arrangement; and (ii) (iii) how the arrangement will vary the effect of the terms; and how the employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of his or her employment as a result of the arrangement; and (iv) states the day on which the arrangement commences. 1.10.4 The employer must give the employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 1.10.5 The employer or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 13 weeks written notice to the other party to the (b) arrangement; or if the Employer and employee agree in writing - at any time. 1.11 CONSULTATION 1.11.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \"Page No. 57 Training / Appointment Requirements SK SK2 SK3 SK4 SK Able to operate and set-up machinery or equipment to manufacture or assemble parts, in both manual and <strong>automated</strong> workstations X X X X X Achieves productivity targets X X X X X Able to identify and report safety issues X X X X X Minimum of 6 months in Moulding and Assembly at Level 1 (SK1) X x X x (or) Minimum of 6 months in Moulding and Assembly prior to cross over from Contract Labour Hire X X X X Able to take action when a safety issue arises X X X X Works under routine supervision either individually or in a team environment X X X X Responsible for the quality of own work X X X X Minimum of 6 months in Moulding and Assembly at Level 2 (SK2) X X x Able to raise a Hazard Report or Incident Report using relevant forms if a safety issue arises X X X Performs work above and beyond the skills of an employee at Level 2 - demonstrated through competence or training X X X Assists in the provision of on the job training to lower\", \n\"Active involvement in collecting, monitoring and analysing data in relation to KPI's (key performance indicators) relevant to the Die setting function X X Be involved and take the lead where required in continuous improvement projects within the moulding area X X Makes recommendations to improve procedures - continuous improvement X X By successful application for vacancy (dependent on the numbers of Die Setters required) X Be actively involved with tooling projects from conception through to commissioning stage X Assist in design of both part and tooling for new projects X Initiate and/or undertake major projects within the mould shop for continuous improvement, such including shop floor setup or <strong>automated</strong> cells X Lead Die Setter team meetings and information sessions and provide support and assistance with any major issues arising within the Mould Shop X Undertake and complete a Diploma in Management or other relevant leadership course to progress to SK8 x Our Ref: Philmac EA 2018_FINAL\"\n)", 
"c(\"Major Change occurs if the employer has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organization, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>.\", \n\"This is a confidential <strong>automated</strong> referral counselling and support service offered to employees to assist with their mental health and wellbeing.\")", 
"c(\"This term applies if the Employer: 10.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 10.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Production SP5 10 Production SP6 10 Production SP7 10 Production Cartridge 10 Production SPOB 10 Production SPPouch 10 Production <strong>Automated</strong> Pigging 10 Production IBC Filling Line 10 Production Pallet Stretch Wrap Station 5 19.7.\")",

"c(\"INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 24.1 This clause applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees. 24.2 Major change (a) For a major change referred to in paragraph 24.1(a): (i) the Company must notify the relevant Employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (ii) subclauses (24.3) to (24.9) apply. 24.3 The relevant Employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this clause. 24.4 If: (a) a relevant Employee appoints, or relevant Employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the Employee or Employees advise the Company of the identity of the representative; the Company must recognise the representative. 9 June 2015\", \n\"Unload and pack trucks/courier vans quality assurance procedures and techniques and identifies basic particularly with 5-axis autoclave curing and components for machine and / or Perform routing, drilling and painting as required quality deviations and faults other <strong>automated</strong> or operale the autoclave riveting Receipt and check inwards goods as process required Interprets technical drawings and accurately robotic machinery Apply paint Place stock that is received in the . completes traceability documentation Prepare moulds .\", \n\"Operate CNC Lathes, autoclave curing and Perform routing, drilling and composite and metallic ISO9001 by adhering to business wide having been assessed as successfully particularly 5-axis operate the autoclave riveting components for and functional QMS procedures completing the necessary assessment training to machine and/or other process painting including liaison and interactions with the requisite standard <strong>automated</strong> or robotic .\")", 
"c(\"(iii) For these reasons the parties to the agreement have agreed to actively work in cooperation with Management of The Austral Brick Company Pty Ltd to achieve its goal of increasing the productivity of Plant personnel to current world's best practice by taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video, and telecommunications as well as computerisation).\", \"Austral Bricks NSW Production Workers Enterprise Agreement 2022 08-06-22 (i) the work is not safe; or (ii) applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or (iii) the work is not appropriate for the employee to perform; or (iv) there are other reasonable grounds for the employee to refuse to comply with the direction. (7) The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 8.3 CONSULTATION Consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultative Arrangements 12.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Employees to whom notice of termination is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Employer is engaged must be given not less than (3) three months' notice of termination.\")", 
"c(\"The Employer will require that group discussions are not held in the main rest areas if it receives requests from employees seeking that said discussions are not held in any of the main rest areas. .. g) The following provisions apply if the Employer: i. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or ii. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Other Defined Work Areas - Skill matrix Testing Skill Matrix Maintenance - Skill Matrix Oil Testing X Preventative maintenance X Routine Testing - LV Tests X Basic machinery breakdown repairs x Routine Testing - HV Tests X Supervise & instruct apprentices X Type Testing X Complex machinery breakdown repairs X Special Testing 0 Oversee minor projects X Test Reporting incl.Type/Special 0 Assist planning and action of difficult/major pri 0 Compliance checking and testing 0 Oversee difficult/major projects 0 Other Activity elective 0 Able to diagnose/repair <strong>automated</strong> equipment 0 .......\"\n)", 
"c(\"Daily working times shall be indelibly recorded by employees and securely maintained on time cards or <strong>automated</strong> recording machine.\", \"24.2 Consultation - Introduction of major change 24.2.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION TERM 11.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Employees agree to submit timesheets electronically via a fully <strong>automated</strong> and logged clock in-clock out timesheet system such as TSheets or similar as implemented by the company. 39.\")", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION TERM 11.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Employees agree to submit timesheets electronically via a fully <strong>automated</strong> and logged clock in-clock out timesheet system such as TSheets or similar as implemented by the company. 39.\")", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 17.1.1 The FAIR WORK REGULATIONS 2009 - SCHEDULE 2.3 will be adopted in this Agreement (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) is seriously considering the introduction of a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Discussions shall include, amongst other things, the reasons for the proposed redundancy (or redundancies), measures to avoid or minimise it (or them), and measures to mitigate the adverse effects of any redundancy or redundancies on the employee(s) concerned. 23.1.4 For the purposes of discussion QWWS Inc. shall as soon as practicable, provide in writing to the employees concerned and the Union all relevant information about the proposed redundancy or redundancies, the number and categories of employees likely to be affected, and the period over which the redundancy or redundancies are likely to be carried out. 23.2 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 23.2.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy that employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 23 of this Agreement, provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by QWWS Inc. of <strong>automation</strong>\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"PLC and <strong>automation</strong> Functionality of industry process . .\")", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION TERM 11.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged, must be given not less than three months notice of termination. 37.8.\")", 
"(b) An <strong>automated</strong> time and attendance system applies.", 
"c(\"41.2 This clause applies if the Company: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Understands and able to operate <strong>automated</strong> central material feeder; Works with minimal supervision and capable of working independently when assigned on a task; .\")", 
"c(\"The parties undertake that there will be no further claims or action in respect of any industrial matters during the operation of this Agreement. 1.7 Consultation 1.7.1 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Performs functions at customer service desk including flow forward, standby, upgrades, and implement delay handling procedures with limited or no guidance Ability to construct and quote complex and/or non <strong>automated</strong> fares and/or taxes and issue all ticketing documentation including manual tickets as required CUSTOMER SERVICE Clarifies specific needs of clients/other employees and follows up where necessary Identifies options for resolution and acts within defined parameters Conducts research as directed regarding customer trends TECHNOLOGY Applies detailed knowledge of various systems and software applications to create and maintain generic databases such as Microsoft Access or Excel Ability to use skills for system backup and support Trains others in the use of office systems and equipment Identifies and/or rectifies minor faults in equipment Creates and maintains generic databases BUSINESS / FINANCIAL Administers cash handling procedures Co-ordinates appointments and related follow-up\"\n)", 
"c(\"Introduction of Change and Redundancy 27.1 Notification of Intended Changes 27.1.1 Where the employer has made a definite decision to implement changes in production, programming, organisation, structure or technology that are likely to have significant effects on employees, the employer shall as soon as practicable notify the employees who may be affected by the proposed changes and the Joint Consultative Committee. 27.1.2 \\\"Significant Effects\\\" include termination of employment; major changes in the composition, operation or size of the employer's workforce or in the skills required; the elimination or diminution of job opportunities, promotion opportunities or job tenure; the alteration of hours of work; the need for retraining or transfer of employees to other work or locations and the restructuring of jobs. 27.2 Consultation Term 27.2.1 (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \"Provided that the employer shall not be required to disclose confidential information or any information that may seriously harm the employer's business undertakings or the employers interest in the carrying on, or disposition, of the business undertaking. 27.4.3 If terminations are unavoidable the employer shall call for volunteers to take redundancy packages, but if this does not produce the necessary reduction in labour determination of the employees to become redundant a selection criteria shall be developed as mutually agreed. 27.5 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 27.5.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by sub-clause 8.5.1 of the Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation\"\n)", 
"c(\"Potential <strong>automation</strong> of the recording process will be investigated within the first six months after certification of this agreement. 25.4 Procedure 25.4.1 Chilled diet (low sugar) cordial will be provided when the average inside temperature reaches 30 degrees Celsius. 25.4.2 When the average inside temperature reaches 35 degrees Celsius, chilled spring water will be issued one per employee per shift period at no charge, during their first heat relief break. 25.4.3.\", \"CONSULTATION IN THE WORKPLACE 34.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Employee Duties At all times, You must: (a) Devote the whole of your time, attention, and skill during your hours of work to the performance and discharge of Your duties; (b) Carry out your duties to the standard Stramit requires; (c) Act honestly, faithfully, and diligently in the performance of your duties; (d) Conform to and comply with Stramit's lawful and reasonable directions; (e) Present for work in appropriate attire; (f) Comply with all Stramit policies and procedures and any other standards developed and communicated to you from time to time; (g) Undertake work-related training as directed by Stramit and comply with all new practices and procedures; (h) Cooperate with changes in work procedures and/or equipment <strong>automation</strong> to improve quality and/or operation; (i) Ensure to the best of your ability that equipment standards and product quality is maintained; (i) Provide Stramit with any information that you have that Stramit may require to assist with any investigations Stramit may\", \n\"Consultation and Major Change 26.1 General (a) This term applies if the employer: (i) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees. 26.2 Obligations regarding consultation (a) For a major change referred to in paragraph 26.1(a)(i) (i) The employer must notify the relevant Employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (ii) Sub-clauses 26.2(c) to (h) apply.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Major Change occurs if the employer has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organization, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>.\", \n\"This is a confidential <strong>automated</strong> referral counselling and support service offered to Employees to assist with their mental health and wellbeing.\")", 
"c(\"Operates at and often exceeds the required bullnose / profile cutting, using advanced cutting machines (Baumer), productivity and quality standards over time, plays a key role in looper operation, small peeler operation, looper set up and housekeeping in the work area, is highly active in safety initiatives troubleshooting, bun handling and storage, Dolphin Pack operation, (e.g. risk assessments, hazard identification, incident investigation) operation of bun management <strong>automation</strong> (Travellator, ABLG1, and makes suggestions for the development of job specific systems ABLG2, BZMA), supervision of chemical unloading, understanding and for the overall improvement of the work area. of foam making chemicals, production line operation, production line sctup, operation of control panels, raw materials and stock control, participating in multi skilling program, data recording for production planning raw materials to be used and stock control ,.\", \n\"APPENDIX C MODEL CONSULTATION TERM (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if Iplex Pipelines Pty Ltd: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Albury Maintenance Classifications and Pay Rates Current as of 31 July 2021 Minimum rates from First Full Pay Period On or After (FFPPOA): Year 1 - 3% Effective Classificatio Year 2 - 3% Year 3 - 3% FFPPOA Lodgement FFPPOA July 31 - 2023 FFPPOA July 31 - 2024 Base Hourly Loaded with FWC - 2022 Rate Rate Base Hourly Loaded Base Hourly Loaded Base Loaded Rate Rate Rate Rate Rate Rate Tradesperson Level 1 $33.3062 $44.0381 $35.2799 $46.6478 $36.3383 $48.0472 $37.4284 $49.4886 (C 8) Tradesperson Level 2 (C7) $34.4817 $45.5925 $36.8836 $48.7682 $37.9901 $50.2313 $39.1298 $51.7382 Tradesperson Level 3 (C6) $37.1388 $49.1058 $40.0908 $53.0089 $41.2935 $54.5991 $42.5323 $56.2371 Advanced Tradesperson $41.6945 N/A $42.9453 $56.7831 $44.2337 $58.4866 $45.5607 $60.2412 (C5) Maintenance / <strong>Automation</strong> Technician $43.2981 N/A $44.5970 $58.9670 $45.9349 $60.7361 $47.3130 $62.5581 C4) Page 43 of 44 Iplex Pipelines Australia Pty Ltd Enterprise Agreement 2022- Albury\"\n)", 
"c(\"shall notify the employer of the employee's intention to resume work after an absence, no later than 2:00 PM on the working day before the day of intended return to work; and 10.5.4 Should any employee attend and offer for work after any absence without first advising the employer in accordance with the requirements of clause 10.5.3 the employer shall not be obliged to employ such employee on that particular day and the employee shall not be entitled to payment for that day if they do not work. 10.6 Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as preventing the employment of any person partly on work in respect of which provision is made by this Agreement and partly on other work, and in such case this Agreement shall apply to the employment of such employee if the major and substantial part of the employee's work is work in respect of which provision is made by this Agreement. 10.7 Use of Time Recording Systems / Time Clocks Where an employee is required to clock on and off using an <strong>automated</strong>\", \n\"Consultation & Introduction of Change 15.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Major Change occurs if the employer has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organization, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>.\", \n\"This is a confidential <strong>automated</strong> referral counselling and support service offered to employees to assist with their mental health and wellbeing.\")", 
"c(\"The parties undertake that there will be no further claims or action in respect of any industrial matters during the operation of this Agreement. 1.7 Consultation 1.7.1 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Ability to construct and quote complex and/or non <strong>automated</strong> fares and/or taxes and issue all ticketing documentation including manual tickets as required CUSTOMER SERVICE Clarifies specific needs of clients/other employees and follows up where necessary Identifies options for resolution and acts within defined parameters Conducts research as directed regarding customer trends TECHNOLOGY · Applies detailed knowledge of various systems and software applications to create and maintain generic databases such as Microsoft Access or Excel Ability to use skills for system backup and support Trains others in the use of office systems and equipment Identifies and/or rectifies minor faults in equipment Creates and maintains generic databases BUSINESS / FINANCIAL Administers cash handling procedures Co-ordinates appointments and related follow-up action Organises business itineraries/meetings and conferences Creates and maintains generic databases Identifying discrepancies and resolving discrepancies\"\n)", 
"$27.48 L3 $28.17 $28.87 $29.59 $26.41 $27.07 $27.75 $28.44 L4 $25.81 $26.46 $27.12 Machinist 1st Class - Spinner $27.80 C10 L1 Spinner with Trade Certificate $29.35 $30.08 $30.83 $31.60 L2 $27.48 $28.17 $28.87 $29.59 L3 $26.41 $27.07 $27.75 LA $28.44 Machinist 1st Class - CNC operator $25.81 $26.46 $27.12 $27.80 C10 L1 $29.35 $30.08 $30.83 $31.60 L2 $27.48 $28.17 $28.87 $29.59 L3 $26.41 $27.07 $27.75 LA $28.44 $25.81 $26.46 $27.12 $27.80 Storeperson C11 L1 $27.83 $28.53 $29.24 $29.97 L2 $26.21 $26.87 $27.54 L3 $28.23 $25.05 $25.68 $26.32 $26.98 L4 $24.46 Machine Operator - Foundry / $25.07 $25.70 $26.34 C11 L1 Forge / Milling $27.08 $27.76 $28.45 $29.16 L2 $25.61 $26.25 $26.91 $27.58 L3 $24.85 $25.47 $26.11 $26.76 L4 $24.46 $25.07 $25.70 $26.34 Machine Operator - Spinner C11 L1 Spinner with in-house training only $27.08 $27.76 $28.45 $29.16 L2 $25.61 $26.25 $26.91 $27.58 L3 $24.85 $25.47 $26.11 $26.76 L4 $24.46 $25.07 $26.34 Machine Operator - Spinner Semi- $25.70 C11 L1 $27.08 $27.76 <strong>Automated</strong>", 
"c(\"The parties agree that consultation will provide Employees with an opportunity to participate fully in decisions which impact on their working environment and conditions. 3.1.1 This term applies if the employer: 3.1.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 3.1.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 3.2 MAJOR CHANGE 3.2.1 For a major change referred to in clause 3.1.1.1: 3.2.1.1 the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the change 3.2.2 The relevant Employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 3.2.3 If: 3.2.3.1 a relevant Employee appoints, or relevant Employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and 3.2.3.2 the Employee or Employees advise the Employer of\", \n\"4.5.2 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.5.2.1 If the services of an Employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such Employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.6.5 provided that Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes must be given not less than three months' notice of termination. 4.5.2.2 Should the Employer fail to give notice of termination as required in clause 4.6 the Employer shall pay to that Employee an amount calculated in accordance with the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation 11.1 This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 11.2 For a major change referred to in paragraph (11.1)(a): a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and b) subclauses (11.3) to (11.9) apply. 11.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 11.4 If a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation, and the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative, the employer must recognise the representative. 11.5 As soon as practicable after making its decision, the employer must\", \n\"Grade 4-Bottling stream (a) Point of entry (i) A Grade 3 employee who has successfully completed training and assessment in: operation and adjustment of equipment that requires a higher level of skill (as specified by the employer;or preparation of filling equipment which includes: - sterilisation and sanitation of filling machines; - sterile wine filtration; - wine transfer; or · preparation and operation of <strong>automated</strong> packer equipment.\")", 
"c(\"The parties agree that consultation will provide Employees with an opportunity to participate fully in decisions which impact on their working environment and conditions. 3.1.1 This term applies if the employer: 3.1.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 3.1.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 3.2 MAJOR CHANGE 3.2.1 For a major change referred to in clause 3.1.1.1: 3.2.1.1 the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the change 3.2.2 The relevant Employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 3.2.3 If: 3.2.3.1 a relevant Employee appoints, or relevant Employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and 3.2.3.2 the Employee or Employees advise the Employer of\", \n\"4.5.2 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.5.2.1 If the services of an Employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such Employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.6.5 provided that Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes must be given not less than three months' notice of termination, 4.5.2.2 Should the Employer fail to give notice of termination as required in clause 4,6 the Employer shall pay to that Employee an amount calculated in accordance with the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 35.1 This term applies if Thai Airways: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"Responsible for securing the arrival, stowage and dispatch of valuable cargo Performing functions at customer service desk including flow forward, standby, upgrades, and implementing delay handling procedures with limited or no guidance Being able to construct and quote complex and/or non-<strong>automated</strong> fares and/or taxes and to issue all ticketing documentation including manual tickets as required A.1.6 Level 4 (a) Characteristics Employees at this level will have achieved a level of Thai Airways or industry specific knowledge sufficient for them to give advice and/or information to Thai Airways and clients in relation to specific areas of their responsibility.\"\n)", 
"c(\"(c) For these reasons the union and its members agree to actively work in co-operation with management of the Austral Brick Company to achieve its goal of increasing the productivity of plant personnel to current worlds best practice by taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video and telecommunications as well as computerisation).\", \"Appendix 4 Consultation (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"A list of co-curricular activities that represent co-curricular contribution by staff will be published and updated from time to time. 7.7 Consultation (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Major change employees. 9\", \n\"\\\"Weeks' Salary\\\" means the ordinary weekly rate of wage for the Teacher concerned. 35.4 Notice Period of Termination on Redundancy (a) If the services of a Teacher are to be terminated due to redundancy, the Teacher shall be entitled to notice of termination as prescribed in clause 33, of this Agreement, unless the reason for termination is due to the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technology changes, and in these cases the notice provided shall not be less than three months.\"\n)", 
"APPENDIX B Performance Incentive 2016 The performance bonus will be based on the following criteria Improved efficiency and daily output - 33% of available bonus Quality standards and reduction in defective parts - 34 % of available bonus Attendance, absenteeism and lost time at work - 33% of available bonus Efficiency - measured monthly - average of 6 monthly totals Standard times (cycle time) have been calculated for all work areas per model, based on current processes and levels of <strong>automation</strong> (see below).", 
"c(\"Consultation regarding Major Workplace Change 9.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Short Course Training includes (but is not limited to) STCW refresher training, DP operator training, maintenance, the <strong>Automation</strong> Electronics and Electrical course and the Helicopter Underwater Escape Training course. 24\")",
