"c(\"EMPLOYEE CONSULTATION 34.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"equivalent Has been employed by the Employer as an Tradesperson for a period of more than 12 months. having sound knowledge/experience of site equipment i.e. wrappers, strappers, etc. can perform routine Industrial maintenance tasks and shift rotation with minimal supervision, has the ability to fault find complex industrial installations, has required qualifications is deemed capable in all of the below fields o designing, planning, making and installing complex mechanical systems, including pneumatic and hydraulic, 0 Fault finding hydraulic and pneumatic systems, o Pressure welding Has good reliability, attitude and flexibility Understands the company quality system Training and mentoring of apprentice's ELECTRICAINS ELECTRICIAN - LEVEL 1 An Electrician - Level 1 is an employee who holds a Victorian A Grade Licence or equivalent Has been employed by the Employer as an Electrician for a period of less than 3 months, and/or has limited breakdown, Industrial maintenance, or industrial <strong>automation</strong>\", \n\"Has a good understanding of the site performs work under limited supervision either individually or in a team environment can perform routine Industrial maintenance tasks and shift rotation without direct supervision, has the ability to fault find complex industrial electrical installations, has required qualifications is deemed capable in 1 of the below fields planning and installing advanced <strong>automation</strong> work, or PLC, HMI and SCADA programming with sound knowledge in Siemens , or has a TUV machinery safety technician level · Has good reliability, attitude and flexibility Understands the company quality system Training and mentoring of apprentice's ELECTRICIAN -- LEVEL 4 An Electrician - Level 4 is an employee who holds a Victorian A Grade Licence or equivalent Has been employed by the Employer as an Electrician for a period of more than 12 months. . can perform routine Industrial maintenance tasks and shift rotation with minimal supervision, has the ability to fault find complex industrial\", \n\"electrical installations, has required qualifications is deemed capable in all of the below fields planning and installing advanced <strong>automation</strong> work, PLC, HMI and SCADA programming with sound knowledge in Siemens , has a TUV machinery safety technician or engineer level Has good reliability, attitude and flexibility Understands the company quality system Training and mentoring of apprentice's APPRENTICES 1ST YEAR APPRENTICE 2ND YEAR APPRENTICE 3RD YEAR APPRENTICE 4TH YEAR APPRENTICE 40.\"\n)", 
"c(\"The nature of the duties includes work of the following nature: basic repetitive work on <strong>automated</strong> or single purpose machines or equipment, e.g. automatic filling. simple machine tinting including dispensing of colorants, operating paint mixing equipment (e.g. via paint shakers or stirrers) and spraying out or drawing down colour samples but where the worker does not match colours by eye and the worker is not trained or authorised to make computerised colour predictions or enter data into colour prediction software and the worker is not authorised to approve tinted colours for colour accuracy (but the worker may approve other quality aspects (e.g. correct product tinted and correct quantity tinted). basic receipt, despatches and inventory work security work routine maintenance work laboratory aide order picking filter operation non licensed electric fork operation c) Paint Worker Grade 3 This classification encompasses the more skilled specialised jobs in the industry.\", \n\"The nature of the duties includes work of the following nature: mill operation dispersion operation raw materials preparation powder coating operations resin manufacturing under supervision operation of all materials handling equipment routine production testing elementary research and development work involving no formal training make up operation fully integrated <strong>automated</strong> filling systems licensed fork-lift operation Colour Matching: . colour matching pigmented colours by eye (excluding stains) in pack sizes of 200 litres or less and exercises this skill on a regular basis OR colour matching pigmented and dye based stains by eye to match customer colour samples supplied on wood and exercises this skill on a regular basis and operates colour prediction software and may be responsible for . updating data in this software and is authorised to approve colour matches. d) Paint Worker Grade 4 This classification encompasses the various skilled jobs in the industry as qualified below.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term apples if the employer: 7.1.1.Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organization, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 7.1.2.\", \n\"Where an employee is required to clock on and off using an <strong>automated</strong> time keeping system, he/she shall on no more than 15 minutes prior to the commencement of their shift, and clock off, without delay, at the end of shift and at the immediate completion of his/her duties. 12.\")", 
"c(\"(8) operating a wide range of complex machines or equipment including large format digital printers, flat bed printers and other printing equipment, <strong>automated</strong> engravers vinyl cutting, engraving equipment and lettering; (9) maintaining records; (10) inspecting products and or materials for conformity with established operational guidelines; (11) using hand trolleys or palate trucks and (where qualified) forklifts, elevated work platforms or cranes; (12) cleaning of floors, outdoor and factory areas; (13) blending paints, resins and tints; (14) occasionally installing signs including (where accredited) complex Installations including working at height and electrical connections in Victoria; (15) regularly installing signs including (where accredited) complex Installations including working at height and electrical connections in states other than Victoria; (16) operating, setting up and adjusting all production machinery in a plant including production process welding to the extent of training\", \n\"Application of this clause (a) This clause 29 applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 29.2.\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION (a) This clause applies if the employer: i. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, programme, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or ii. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 6.1 Major change: (a) For a major change referred to in clause 6(a)(i): i. the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and ii. subclause 6.1 applies.\", \n\"(e) If the services of a Teacher are to be terminated due to redundancy due restructure, the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technology changes, the notice provided shall not be less than three (3) months. 33.2 Notice - Operations Employees (a) Except in the case of casual or probationary employees, where the contract of employment is terminated prior to the conclusion of the specified contract end date, a minimum of four (4) weeks' notice is required by either party.\", \n\"(d) If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy due to restructure, the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technology changes, the notice provided shall not be less than three (3) months. 33.3 Job Search Entitlement i.\")", 
"Working of clinker stockpile within the clinker shed to ensure continuous supply to the Mill Loading of Limestone and gypsum into appropriate hoppers to ensure continuous supply to the Mill Understand the bulk loading system including providing assistance to transport operators using the <strong>automated</strong> weighbridge loading system and off-bridge loading.", 
"Paper rosters, until such time that they are JM <strong>automated</strong>, will be signed by the supervisor and an employee representative at the time of issue.", 
"Working of clinker stockpile within the clinker shed to ensure continuous supply to the Mill Loading of Limestone and gypsum into appropriate hoppers to ensure continuous supply to the Mill Understand the bulk loading system including providing assistance to transport operators using the <strong>automated</strong> weighbridge loading system and off-bridge loading.", 
"c(\"(a) basic repetitive work on <strong>automated</strong> or single purpose machines or equipment, eg. automatic filling (b) simple machine tinting (c) basic receipt, despatch and inventory work (d) security work (e) routine maintenance work (f) order picking (g) filter press operation (h) non licensed electric fork operation 20.3 Paint Worker Grade 3 20.3.1 This classification encompasses the more skilled specialised jobs in the industry. 20.3.2 An employee in this classification shall work under general routine supervision, be responsible for their own work and have completed or be involved in training so as to enable the employee to perform work within the scope of this grade. 20.3.3 A grade 3 paint worker shall perform work falling within the lower grades and be proficient in duties for which they have been trained within a site's functional stream(s).\", \n\"The nature of the duties includes work of the following nature: (a) mill operation (b) dispersion operation (c) raw materials preparation (d) powder coating operations (e) resin manufacturing under supervision (f) operation of all materials handling equipment (g) tinting under supervision (h) routine production testing (i) elementary research and development work involving no formal training (j) make up operation (k) fully integrated <strong>automated</strong> filling systems 20.4 Paint Worker Grade 4 20.4.1 This classification encompasses the various skilled jobs in the industry.\"\n)", 
"7.2 Base rates for Swap 'n' Go staff employed in <strong>Automated</strong> High Capacity Plants: Level Position Description $/hr $/hr $/hr 01/09/16 01/09/17 01/09/18 1 Yard Hand, not involved in Plant operation $21.5832 $22.0149 $22.4552 2 Scraper/ Primary grader, hanger and $22.5114 $22.9616 $23.4209 loader 3 Cylinder Filler $22.7460 $23.2009 $23.6649 4 Advanced Filler * $23.1540 $23.6171 $24.0894 OR Forklift (licensed) part time 5 Advanced filler * + forklift (licensed) part $23.6130 $24.0853 $24.5670 time 6 Advanced filler with ability to operate all $24.4086 $24.8968 $25.3947 equipment ** + non signatory retester (out of date cylinders) *** OR Forklift driver Full time 7 Level 6 skills + deputy leading hand $25.0920 $25.5938 $26.1057 8 Active leading hand with all skills $26.3772 $26.9047 $27.4428 S'n'G Cylinder Truck Drivers Flat top above 4.5 t GVM and below 8 t $26.2752 $26.8007 $27.3367 GVM Flat top above 8 t GVM $27.6012 $28.1532 $28.7163 Cage Installer / runabout / cylinder delivery (less than", 
"NULL", "c(\"9.2.2 A grade 2 Paint Worker shall perform work falling within the lower grade and be proficient in duties for which they have been trained within a site's functional stream(s). 9.2.3 The nature of the duties in this Grade includes work of the following nature: (a) basic repetitive work on <strong>automated</strong> or single purpose machines or equipment, eg. automatic filling; (b) simple machine tinting; (c) basic receipt, despatch and inventory work; (d) security work; (e) routine maintenance work; (f) laboratory aide; (g) order picking; (h) filter press operation; and (i) non licensed electric fork operation. 9.3 Paint Worker Grade 3 9.3.1 This classification encompasses the more skilled specialised jobs in the industry. 9.3.2 An employee in this classification will work under general routine supervision, be responsible for their own work and have completed or be involved in training so as to enable the employee to perform work within the scope of this grade. 9.3.3 A grade 3 Paint Worker will perform\", \n\". () make up operation; and (k) fully integrated <strong>automated</strong> filling systems. 9.4 Paint Worker Grade 4 9.4.1 This classification encompasses the various skilled jobs in the industry. 9.4.2 An employee in this classification will be generally responsible for their own work, have completed training so as to enable the employee to perform work within the scope of this grade and be able to perform work from complex instructions and procedures. 9.4.3 A grade 4 paint worker shall perform work falling within the lower grades for which they have been trained within a site's functional stream(s) and be proficient in duties of the following nature: (a) bulk paint tinting; and (b) resin manufacturing 9.5 Team Leader Grade 5 An employee appointed by the Employer to this position shall be responsible as follows: (a) performance appraisal; (b) quality control; (c) production control; (d) training of other employees; (e) safety management; and (f) acceptability for manufacturing process. 9.6 Resin Assistant\"\n)", 
"c(\"Wingham Beef Exports Pty Limited - Enterprise Agreement, 2019 9 CONSULTATION & INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 9.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"notify the employer of the employee's intention to resume work after an absence, no later than 2:00 PM on the working day before the day of intended resumption of work; and 18.5.4 Should any employee attend an offer for work after any absence without first advising the employer in accordance with the requirements of clause 18.5.3 the employer shall not be obliged to employ such employee on that particular day and the employee shall not be entitled to payment for that day if they are not employed. 18.6 Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as preventing the employment of any person partly on work in respect of which provision is made by this Agreement and partly on other work, and in such case this Agreement shall apply to the employment of such employee if the major and substantial part of the employee's work is work in respect of which provision is made by this Agreement. 18.7 Use of Time Recording Systems / Time Clocks Where an employee is required to clock on and off using an <strong>automated</strong>\"\n)", 
"c(\"23 CONSULTATION ON CHANGE (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Training Reporting Production Learning & Development And take advantage of <strong>automation</strong> of the reinforcing steel manufacturing industry.\")", 
"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.", 
"NULL", "c(\"Consultation regarding Major Workplace Change 9.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Short Course Training includes (but is not limited to) STCW refresher training, DP operator training, maintenance, the <strong>Automation</strong> Electronics and Electrical course and the Helicopter Underwater Escape Training course.\")", 
"c(\"(1) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (2) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"<strong>Automated</strong> (PC controlled) Their performance is measured on safety, quality, team management and throughput.\")", 
"c(\"This term applies if the employer: a. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"GSTEC Pty Limited Single Enterprise Agreement 2023 SCHEDULE E - TRANSITION ARRANGEMENTS FOR EXISTING EMPLOYEES Greenstar Building <strong>Automation</strong> Pty Limited GSTEC Pty Limited Single Enterprise Single Enterprise Agreement 2016 Agreement 2023 Classification Classification Electrical Worker Grade 5 Unlicenced Electrical Worker Grade 5 Unlicenced Electrical Worker Grade 5 Licenced Electrical Worker Grade 5 Licenced Electrical Worker Grade 6 Electrical Worker Grade 6 Electrical Worker Grade 7 Electrical Worker Grade 7 Electrical Worker Grade 8 Electrical Worker Grade 8 34\"\n)", 
"c(\"(ifi) For these reasons the employees and the Unions agree to actively work in cooperation with the management of the Company at Camellia to identify and implement incremental continuous improvement initiatives which achieve their goals of increasing the productivity of manufacturing and distribution personnel to current worlds best practice by not only taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video and telecommunications as well as computerisation), but also improving the efficiency of existing technology and systems by targeting waste and downtime elimination.\", \n\"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation and Communication 3.1.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"The general obligation of the employer will be no more than to give such employees and their chosen representatives, including the union, an indication of the impending redundancy at the first reasonable opportunity, and to take such steps as must be reasonable to facilitate the obtaining by such employees of suitable alternative employment. b) This clause does not apply where employment is terminated as a consequence of conduct that at common law justifies instant dismissal or in the case of casual employees or employees engaged for a specific period of time or for a specified task or tasks. 4.8.3 Period of notice of termination on redundancy a) If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy the employee will be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.8 - Termination of employment. b) Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other\"\n)", 
"This vision is characterised by: > a lean operation with a high degree of <strong>automation</strong>, > highly skilled employees who are well remunerated, > a responsive business with fast changeovers and fast product development, > a workforce who take responsibility for their own performance and the performance of their shift team and section, > a flexible workforce, with flexibility between function, other departments, and roster arrangements that cover five to seven day operations.", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION TERM 1.11.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"25 36.4 Clause 36.5 does not apply where employment is terminated as a consequence of conduct that at common law justifies instant dismissal or in the case of casual employees or employees engaged for a specific period of time or for a specified task or tasks. 36.5 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy the employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 37 - Termination of employment. 36.6 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged must be given not less than three months notice of termination. 36.7 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in Clauses 36.5 or 36.6 the employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required\"\n)", 
"c(\"There will be no bans, stoppages or limitations on the way that work is customarily performed. 13.6 At any stage an employee may seek help, assistance or representation from a person, agent or union of their choice. 14.0 INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 14.1 General (a) This clause applies if the employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"reasons for the proposed terminations; (b) the number and categories of employees likely to be affected; (c) the number of workers normally employed; and (d) The period over which the terminations are likely to be carried out. 21.2 Method of selection and procedure for redundancy In the first instance, the employer shall call for volunteers from the area where surplus labour exists: 21.2.1 In the event of insufficient volunteers the employer reserves the right to select employees for redundancy. 21.2.2 In the event of excessive volunteers, the employer will select the persons from that list of volunteers to be made redundant. 21.3 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 21.3.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 20.2. 21.3.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong>\"\n)", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 1.11.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (1) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"(b) Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged must be given not less than three months notice of termination.\")", 
"c(\"(c) For these reasons the union and its members agree to actively work in co-operation with management of the Austral Brick Company to achieve its goal of increasing the productivity of plant personnel to current world's best practice by taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video and telecommunication s as well as computerisation).\", \"Appendix 4 Consultation (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation regarding major workplace change Model Consultation Term (regulation 2.09) (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Provided that Engineers to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes will be given not less than three month's notice of termination. 42.3.\")", 
"c(\"Part 3 - Consultation and Communication and Dispute Settlement Procedure 3.1 Consultation and Communication 3.1.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"No employer is required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be against the employer's interests. 4.4.4 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.4.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.3. 4.4.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of employment is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged must be given not less than three months' notice of termination. 4.4.4.3 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 4.3.1.1 or 4.3.1.2 the employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"c(\"This term applies if the Employer: 6.1.1. has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 6.1.2. proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Complete detailed documentation, Use parts, PLC programming, Robot/<strong>Automation</strong> Experience). electrical data entry devices- intermediate skills for entry, retrieval and manipulation.\", \"This includes the ability to use PLC programs and use PLC programs and software to fault find and ensure issues pertaining to such Compliance: Ensure all work complies with local, state, and software to fault find and ensure issues pertaining to such devices devices are resolved, Program new PLCs, Program writing for existing PLCs, backups, national electrical codes and safety regulations. are resolved <strong>Automation</strong> background for Robotics etc.\", \n\"Use Machine & Technical voltage systems, PLCs, PLC Programming, Program writing, backups, <strong>Automation</strong>, Line Knowledge intermediate skills for entry, retrieval and manipulation. electrical data entry devices - intermediate skills for entry, retrieval Control, Requires advanced knowledge and skills in their specialty.\")", 
"c(\"22.4 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 22.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy that employee must be given notice of termination as set out in sub-clause 20.1, or otherwise paid in lieu, as set out in sub-clause 20.2. 22.4.2 If the company makes payment instead of notice for all or any of the period of notice, then the period for which the payment is made must be treated as service for the purposes of calculating any service related entitlements of the employee arising in accordance with this enterprise agreement and is considered to be service with the company for the purposes of the Long Service Leave Act and the accrual of annual leave. 22.5 Period of notice of termination on redundancy because of <strong>automation</strong> or technological change 22.5.1 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in relation\", \n\"to which the company is engaged must be given not less than three months specific notice of termination. 22.5.2 <strong>Automation</strong> is defined as when new machinery does not require the use of labour.\", \"<strong>Automation</strong> and technological change, for example, is not the installation of a new bottling line or a new model of equipment.\", \"FOR EXAMPLE THE COMPANY WANTS TO INTRODUCE <strong>AUTOMATION</strong>, SUCH AS ROBOTICS, TO A PARTICULAR DEPARTMENT.\")", 
"c(\"CONSULTATION 35.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 35.2 Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (35.1) (a): (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (35.3) to (35.9) apply. 35.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 35.4 If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; 18\", \n\"GPS Scheduling Automatic Weighing <strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection Other Technological Changes Management shall involve employees in discussions regarding the introduction of technological change.\")", 
"c(\"SeaRoad Shipping Enterprise Agreement Stevedoring 2018 SCHEDULE 7 - MOORING AND UNMOORING 1 The Company and the Union accept that the introduction of <strong>automated</strong> mooring systems for the Company's vessels has removed the need for stevedoring employees to perform mooring and unmooring (\\\"mooring\\\") work in Webb Dock and Devonport in normal circumstances. 2 Notwithstanding the above, it is agreed that sufficient employees will make themselves available for mooring work as required in emergent circumstances at these ports.\", \n\"SCHEDULE 12 CONSULTATION Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"37 PRIVATE HEALTH INSURANCE 25 38 SUPERANNUATION 26 39 SUPERANNUATION SALARY SACRIFICE 27 40 MINIMUM SALARIES AND NEW VESSELS 28 41 RECORD KEEPING 28 42 LEAVE 29 43 RECOVERY OF LEAVE IN ADVANCE 29 44 ACCUMULATION OF LEAVE FOR STUDY PURPOSES 30 45 PARENTAL LEAVE . 30 46 LONG SERVICE LEAVE 31 47 COMPASSIONATE LEAVE. 37 48 JURY SERVICE. 38 49 CONVEYANCE. 38 50 COMPANY SEMINARS / TRAINING / TRAINING ON LEAVE 38 51 OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY TRAINING 39 52 <strong>AUTOMATION</strong>, ELECTRONICS AND ELECTRICAL TRAINING 39 53 STCW '95 TRAINING COURSES 39 54 PAYMENT AND EXPENSES WHILE TRAINING 39 55 STUDY ALLOWANCE 39 56 TRAINEE ENGINEER / CADET TRAINING 41 57 VESSELS UNDER OVERHAUL OR DOCKING 44 58 TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT BY THE EMPLOYER. 45 59 TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT BY THE EMPLOYEE. 46 60 SEVERANCE PAY 46 61 PAYMENT ON TERMINATION . 47 62 PAYMENT OF LEAVE ON TERMINATION OF EMPLOYMENT 47 63 COMPENSATION FOR LOSS OF CERTIFICATE OF COMPETENCY 47 64 EMPLOYEE CONSULTATIVE COMMITTEE 50 65 DISPUTES SETTLEMENT\", \n\"Such training does not include training for the purpose of accreditation or skills maintenance as referred to in Clauses 51, 52, 53, 55 and 56. 51 OCCUPATIONAL HEALTH AND SAFETY TRAINING 51.1 Occupational Health and Safety training will be consistent with Section 47 of the Occupational Health and Safety (Maritime Industry) Act, 1993 (as amended). 52 <strong>AUTOMATION</strong>, ELECTRONICS AND ELECTRICAL TRAINING 52.1 The Employer remains committed to a course of electrical training or similar content to the course known as the <strong>Automation</strong>, Electronic, & Electrical (AEE) Course, of 5 weeks duration.\"\n)", 
"c(\"INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 12.1 This term applies if Hazeldene's: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"pre-pack presenter and evisceration checker placing a whole bird and/or pieces into a plastic bag and/or clipping and/or placing the bagged or bulk bird into a carton or crate to the quality standards as determined by the Hazeldene's · placing a bird/pieces into a plastic bag and/or clipping the bag on an automatic or semiautomatic machine, or an employee who rotates between this work and that mentioned above being packing of fresh whole birds and/or pieces into cartons, tubs or crates sorting and selecting (and trimming where necessary) pieces of boneless product to achieve random/set weights on valumatic trays and presenting the product to quality specifications which includes no blemishes, no retention of viscera, no protrusions or overlap and to a standard specification layout grading of birds to ABC quality standards (grader machine) experienced in all duties related to 9 piece cut up machine in order to consistently achieve Hazeldene's quality standards . feeding product to the <strong>automated</strong>\"\n)", 
"R SEE <strong>Automation</strong> .", "c(\"Yours sincerely David Crosby General Manager AUTOMATIC FIRE PROTECTION Environmental OAIC EXCELL Monarch BAS DATATECH <strong>Automation</strong> ALES AIC SOLUTIONS BEALEGT CONTROL -- C:\\\\Active\\\\WorkIndustrial Relations\\\\EBAIEBA 2016\\\\Fire AlarmsAsset Fire Documents\\\\Asset Fire FWC letter 14.02.2017.docx\", \"CONSULTATION (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"ermironmental <strong>Automation</strong>\")", 
"c(\"(iii) For these reasons the employees and the Unions agree to actively work in cooperation with the management of the Company at Camellia to identify and implement incremental continuous improvement initiatives which achieve their goals of increasing the productivity of manufacturing and distribution personnel to current worlds best practice by not only taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video and telecommunications as well as computerisation), but also improving the efficiency of existing technology and systems by targeting waste and downtime elimination.\", \n\"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"NULL", "c(\"CONSULTATION 35.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) proposes to make a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 35.2 Major change For a major change referred to in paragraph (35.1) (a) and prior to implementing the decision and major change: (a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the proposed decision to introduce the major change; and (b) subclauses (35.3) to (35.9) apply. 35.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 35.4 If: (a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and (b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; the employer must recognise\", \n\"GPS Scheduling GPS Automatic Weighing <strong>Automation</strong> of Trip Selection Electronic Work Diaries (EWD) Camera units that capture in cab, forward and side views Other Technological Changes .\")", 
"c(\"The parties undertake to resolve any disputes in a timely manner in accordance with the disputes procedure. 2.2 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 2.2.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) Has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) Proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"For example: Industrial <strong>Automation</strong> and Control, Industrial Electronics and Control, Fire Protection, Refrigeration Mechanic, Machine Learning, loT sensors, etc. 48\")", 
"c(\"uniQue METALS LASER 49 Consultation 49.1 This term applies if the employer: a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. c) Major change 49.2 For a major change referred to in paragraph (36.1) (a): a) the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and b) subclauses (36.3) to (36.9) apply. 49.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 49.4 lf: a) a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and b) the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative. c) the employer must recognise the representative. 49.5 As soon as practicable\", \n\"Meet requirements of Level 1 plus: · Can performs technology changes and carry out basic adjustments Carry out SOD Calibrations Can start up and shut down machines Can change laser gas bottles Can change technologies in programs Can set up job lists and <strong>automation</strong> Can achieve acceptable cutting quality on 25mm Mild Steel and 20mm Stainless Steel Can identify improvement opportunities in programming.\")", 
"NULL", "c(\"SCHEDULE 7 - MOORING AND UNMOORING - The Company and the Union accept that the introduction of <strong>automated</strong> mooring systems for the Company's vessels has removed the need for stevedoring Employees to perform mooring and unmooring (\\\"mooring\\\") work in Webb Dock and Devonport in normal circumstances. 2 Notwithstanding the above, it is agreed that sufficient Employees will make themselves available for mooring work as required in emergent circumstances at these ports.\", \"SCHEDULE 12 CONSULTATION Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the Employer; (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation regarding major workplace change Model Consultation Term (regulation 2.09) (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Provided that Engineers to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes will be given not less than three month's notice of termination. 42.3 Severance Pay 42.3.1In addition to any period of notice prescribed for ordinary termination in this Award, and subject to further order of the Commission, an employee whose employment is terminated for reasons set out herein shall be entitled to the following minimum amount of severance pay in respect of a continuous period of service: Period of continuous service Severance Pay Less than 1 years service Nil 1 year and less than 2 years 4 weeks 2 years and less than 3 years 6 weeks 3 years and less than 4 years 7 weeks 4 years and less than 5 years 8 weeks 5 years and less than 6 years 10 weeks 6 years and less than 7 years 11 weeks 7 years and less than 8 years 13 weeks 8 years and less than 9 years 14 weeks 9 years\"\n)", 
"c(\"(1) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (2) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"<strong>Automated</strong> (PC controlled) Their performance is measured on safety, quality, team management and throughput.\")", 
"Operate a computerised Concrete Batching Plant and <strong>automated</strong> bin fill system. 52.9.1.", 
"c(\"Breaches of this policy will result in disciplinary action being taken 3.8 Mobile Phones The use of mobile phones is not allowed in the workshop during work times, as this can affect <strong>automated</strong> machinery and distract persons while operating machinery.\", \"9 Consultation (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"The employer or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or (b) if the employer and employee agree in writing - at any time. (7) The right to make an agreement pursuant to this clause is in addition to, and is not intended to otherwise affect, any provision for an agreement between the company and an individual employee contained in any other term of this agreement. 8 MODEL CONSULTATION CLAUSE (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Indicative duties carried out to the processing and site operations for a Maintenance Electrical Stream Grade 3 employee include: <strong>Automation</strong> Programming PLC and HMI including SCADA systems Servo Programming 6 Instrumentation Calibration \\200 Line Control Certified Electrical Team Leader RCA Training Robot Recipe Programmer TP <strong>Automation</strong> Certified Filler/DE Certified (or Equivalent) able to complete all major servicing and Fault find equipment in a timely manner Process Certified (or equivalent) able to complete all major servicing and Fault Find Equipment in a timely manner Pactum Dairy Shepparton EA 2018 41\"\n)", 
"R SEE <strong>Automation</strong> .", "Such duties include, but are not limited to: Cleaning; housekeeping; picking; packing; replenishing; loading and unloading of vehicles; operation of computer equipment; operation of licensed or non-licensed materials handling equipment; acceptance and despatch of goods; operation of <strong>automated</strong> storage and retrieval systems; operation of Radio Frequency (RF) equipment; container de-stuffing, administrative work; assisting in the on job training of Team Members in Training, and other duties as directed. 2.2.3 Level 3 - Team Coordinator The Company may appoint a Team Coordinator who is required to perform the full range of operational tasks in accordance with business needs but is also required to support the Team Leader in various team management functions.", 
"c(\"Consultation regarding major workplace change Model Consultation Term (regulation 2.09) (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Provided that Engineers to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes will be given not less than three month's notice of termination. 45.5 Severance Pay 45.5.1 In addition to any period of notice prescribed for ordinary termination in this Award, and subject to further order of the Commission, an employee whose employment is terminated for reasons set out herein shall be entitled to the following minimum amount of severance pay in respect of a continuous period of service: Period of continuous service Severance pay Less than 1 year Nil 1 year and less than 2 years 4 weeks' pay 2 years and less than 3 years 6 weeks' pay 3 years and less than 4 years 7 weeks' pay 4 years and less than 5 years 8 weeks' pay 5 years and less than 6 years 10 weeks' pay 6 years and less than 7 years 11 weeks' pay 7 years and less than 8 years 13 weeks' pay 8 years and less than\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation Regarding Major Workplace Change 13.1 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Time and Attendance 57.3 It is a requirement of employment that all staff must use our <strong>automated</strong> time and attendance system.\")", 
"c(\"in the composition, operation or size of the Company's workforce or in the skills required; (d) the elimination or diminution of job opportunities (including promotion opportunities or job tenure); (e) the alteration of hours of work; (f) the need for retraining or transfer of Employees to other work or locations; and (g) the restructuring of jobs. 32.2.2 The Company, the Employees and the Union will support the SCC's activities and its outcomes. 32.2.3 The SCC will be responsible for overseeing the implementation of this Agreement at the site and to ensure that the agreed Aims and Objectives are achieved. 32.2.4 The SCC has established a constitution to guide the conduct of the SCC to be a representative body to enable it to achieve the Aims and Objectives of this Agreement. 33 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE AND ROSTERS 33.1 This term applies if the Employer (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. .\", \"(g) Fault finding/root cause analysis (h) Knowledge of <strong>automated</strong> processes 47.3 Wage Group C8: - Engineering/Manufacturing Tradesperson-Special Class Level I An Engineering/Manufacturing Tradesperson-Special Class Level I means a: (a) Special Class Engineering Tradesperson (Electrical/Electronic)-Level I; or (b) Special Class Engineering Tradesperson (Mechanical)-Level I; or (c) Special Class Engineering Tradesperson (Fabrication)-Level I; or (d) equivalent.\"\n)", 
"c(\"(R SEF <strong>Automation</strong> .\", \"O SEE <strong>Automation</strong> .\")", 
"c(\"FOODMACH Inspired <strong>Automation</strong> Solutions Foodmach Pty.\", \"Appendix 1 -Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"FOODMACH Inspired <strong>Automation</strong> Solutions palletising - depallatiling - rotintins . conveying Foodmach Pty.\")", 
". * Other fully <strong>automated</strong> processes as they come on line.", 
"The Company's effectiveness in this will result from continued input by all employees and acknowledgement and acceptance of relevant responsibilities by all parties concerned. 3.4 An environment of mutual honesty and trust will be complemented by business like behaviour and mutual respect of and by all parties gained through consultation and communications. 3.5 In support of maintaining a multi skilled workforce, <strong>automated</strong> machinery and accommodating cyclic loadings within the factory, it is accepted that employees will be required to work in a section other than their normal area of work. 3.6 With the achievement of the above items, the parties will enhance customer relationships and thus promote The Company's ability to win additional business aimed at ensuring longevity of employment and a profitable business. 4.", 
"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program. organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.", 
"Reading, interpreting and calculating information from production drawings, prints or plans in order to complete specific tasks; (B) Preparing quotes and tenders; (C) Assembling components using written, spoken and/or diagrammatic instructions; (D) Performing duties and using tools specific to their particular trade; (E) Exercising high precision trade skills using various materials and/or specialist techniques; (F) Finishing process include coating, painting, spray-painting, colouring, varnishing, lacquering, enamelling, oxidising, glazing, galvanising, electroplating, gilding, bronzing, engraving, cleaning, polishing and finishing of any of such signs in paint, decorative or protective surface coatings or coverings (G) Assisting in the provision of on the job training in conjunction with other tradespersons, Non Tradespersons and Supervisors; (H) Operating a wide range of complex machines or equipment including large format digital printers, flat bed printers and other printing equipment, <strong>automated</strong>", 
"c(\"Employment Relationship ... .7 2.1 Contract of Employment 7 2.2 <strong>Automation</strong> 9 2.3 Redundancy ... 10 2.4 No Detriment .. 11 3.\", \"., the Company may deduct from the final wages the reasonable value of the article. 2.1 10 Employees Presenting Themselves for Work and Not Required - Subject to the provisions of this sub-clause 2.1 Contract of Employment, an employee who presents for overtime work without notice that they will not be required shall be paid at least four hours' pay. 2.2 <strong>Automation</strong> 2.2.1 Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause 2.1, Contract of Employment, where, on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of mechanisation of technological changes in the industry covered by this award, the Company proposes to terminate the employment of an employee covered by this Agreement who has been employed by it for the preceding 12 months, it shall give the employee three months' notice of the termination of employment.\"\n)", 
"Proven ability in the design, installation, commissioning and expedient fault finding of low scale <strong>automation</strong> processes and proficiency in site hydraulic services; OR . proven ability in the design, installation, commissioning and expedient fault finding of low scale <strong>automation</strong> processes and SLC systems and proficiency in analog functions Individuals with Advanced Diplomas shall be considered for salaried positions.",

"c(\"CONSULTATION AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION 11.1 Model consultation clause 11.1.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Grade Three Grade two, plus Grade two, plus (3) An employees who can successfully operate in Permanent Employee who can successfully one of the following positions: complete (any) of the following key warehouse forklift tasks according to warehouse Line Leader on one production line procedures: <strong>Automated</strong> Case Packing Operator Sewing Operator *All facets of Receiving, including receiving line Print room Operator docks and flatbeds including putaways using RF Blending room Operator & Store equipment Assistant (including forklift licence) *All facets of Picking with LO license for Turret/High Reach picking/Stock Picker Single Machine operation *All facets of Online & Shop Quilter/Card/Blender/Operate complex *All facets of Despatching equipment including Computing skills.\"\n)",

"s (SS1 & SS2) An Employee who has multiple trade qualifications which are utilised by the company and /or has been qualified by the Company to perform work that requires particular specialist skills such as Rotor Balancing, Ground hop, <strong>Automated</strong> Shot Peening, NDT Level 1 & 2, Dangerous Goods Licenced or other skills as determined by the Company and will be paid the applicable SS allowance as prescribed in clause 9.",
