"Provided that G4S shall not be required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which, when looked at objectively, would be inimical to the interests of G4S. 6.6.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy (i) If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, then such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 6.1.1 of this Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by G4S of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which G4S is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination.", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "c(\"CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 11.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Technical Officer (MC or ARM) - no qualification $70,000 Technical Officer (MC or ARM) - degree qualified $83,500 Manufacturing Supervisor (no qualification) $85,000 Professional - Entry Level Scientists & IT (Level 1 - 3 year degree) $64,800 Professional - Entry Level Scientists & IT (Level 1 - 4/5 year degree) $66,400 Professional - Entry Level Scientists & IT (Level 2) $67,500 Professional - Entry Level Scientists & IT (Level 3) $70,400 Professional - Entry Level Scientists & IT (Level 4) $73,900 Professional - Scientist & IT $76,400 Professional - Experienced Scientist & IT $83,500 Professional - Technical Expert (Scientist & IT) $100,000 Professional Supervisor $95,000 Engineering Trades $75,000 Engineers -- Entry Level (Level 1 - 3 year degree) $64,800 Engineers - Entry Level (Level 1 - 4/5 year degree) $66,400 Engineers - Entry Level (Level 2) $67,500 Engineers - Entry Level (Level 3) $70,400 Engineers - Entry Level (Level 4) $73,900 Engineers $80,000 Experienced Engineers $100,000 <strong>Automation</strong>\"\n)", 
"c(\"EXTRACTAS BIOSCIENCE (iii) how the employee will be better off overall in relation to the terms and conditions of his or her employment as a result of the arrangement; and (e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. (4) The employer must give the employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. (5) The employer or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or (b) if the employer and employee agree in writing-at any time. 5 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE Fair Work Regulations 2009 - Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (Regulation 2.09) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>\", \n\"Classifications Minimum Salary Security Officer $55,000 ASO $52,000 D&RM Specialist $60,000 Executive Assistant $65,000 Sales & Shipping Co-ordinator $75,000 Supply Officer $50,000 Manufacturing Operator $55,000 Manufacturing Operator - Experienced $65,000 Manufacturing Technician - Cannabis $50,000 Manufacturing Technician - Cannabis - Experienced $65,000 Supply Co-ordinator $55,000 Manufacturing Co-ordinator $70,000 Maintenance Non-Trade $48,000 Mechanical Trade $60,000 Electrical Trade $70,000 Maintenance - Experienced $70,000 Maintenance - Technical Expert $75,000 Maintenance - Team Leader $85,000 Maintenance - Planner $75,000 MC Tech Officer $80,000 Professional - Entry Level Scientists & IT $55,000 Professional - Scientist & IT $65,000 Professional - Experienced Scientist & IT $80,000 Professional - Technical Expert (Scientist & IT) $100,000 Professional Supervisor $95,000 Engineering Trades $75,000 Engineers - Entry Level $60,000 Engineers $80,000 Experienced Engineers $100,000 <strong>Automation</strong>\"\n)", 
"LINFOX NEW SOUTH WALES (SNACKBRANDS WAREHOUSE - NUW) AGREEMENT 2017 3.11.4 The employer must give the employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within fourteen (14) days after it is agreed to. 3.11.5 The employer or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than twenty-eight (28) days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or (b) if the employer and employee agree in writing - at any time. 3.12 CONSULTATION 3.12.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.", 
"be performed by any other employee of FUSF, and redundant has a corresponding meaning. 3.5.2 Exclusions This clause does not apply: (a) where employment is terminated as a consequence of serious misconduct; (b) to Casual employees. 3.5.3 Discussions before Termination Where FUSF has made a decision that FUSF no longer requires the job an employee has been doing to be performed by the individual or by any other employee of FUSF and that decision may lead to termination of employment, FUSF will have discussions as soon as practicable with the employee directly affected and, if requested by the employee, with their Union, to consider all options. 3.5.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy a) If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by this Agreement. b) Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given due to the introduction or proposed introduction by FUSF of <strong>automation</strong>", 
"BlueScope Distribution Enterprise Agreement Granville 2021 - 2025 16.3 Skills Payment Table Senior Operator Skills: Q $28.92 $29.50 $30.09 $30.69 $31.30 R $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 Machine Operator Skills (Slitter Operation): M $13.74 $14.01 $14.29 $14.58 $14.87 M Basic $7.01 $7.15 $7.29 $7.44 $7.59 P $28.92 $29.50 $30.09 $30.69 $31.30 L $28.92 $29.50 $30.09 $30.69 $31.30 K $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 N $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 Machine Operator Skills (Shearline Operation): F $13.74 $14.01 $14.29 $14.58 $14.87 F Basic $7.01 $7.15 $7.29 $7.44 $7.59 J $28.92 $29.50 $30.09 $30.69 $31.30 G $28.92 $29.50 $30.09 $30.69 $31.30 H $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 E $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 Recoiler: T $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 <strong>Automated</strong> Packing Line: U $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 Store/Machine Assistant: D $20.75 $21.17 $21.59 $22.02 $22.46 16 of 31", 
"c(\"Part 3 - Consultation and Communication and Dispute Settlement Procedure 3.1 Consultation and Communication 3.1.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"4.4.4 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.4.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.3. 4.4.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of employment is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged must be given not less than three months' notice of termination. 4.4.4.3 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 4.3.4.1 or 4.3.4.2 the employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Consultation 7.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) is proposing to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Packaging methods include manual processes, semi-automatic and fully <strong>automated</strong> packaging equipment.\")", 
"Provided that any employer shall not be required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be inimical to the employee's interests. 5.2.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 5.1.1 of this Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination.", 
"Such duties Include, but are not limited to: Cleaning; housekeeping; picking; packing; replenishing; loading and unloading of vehicles; operation of computer equipment; operation of licensed or non-licensed materials handling equipment; acceptance and despatch of goods; operation of <strong>automated</strong> storage and retrieval systems; operation of Radio Frequency (RF) equipment; administrative work; on job training and assessment of Team Members in Training, and other dutles as directed.", 
"c(\"Consultation 7.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) is proposing to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Packaging methods include manual processes, semi-automatic and fully <strong>automated</strong> packaging equipment.\")", 
"c(\"(c) For these reasons the union and its members agree to actively work in co-operation with management of the Austral Brick Company to achieve its goal of increasing the productivity of plant personnel to current world's best practice by taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video, and telecommunication s as well as computerization).\", \"Appendix 4 Consultation (I) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"employment as a result of the arrangement; and (e) states the day on which the arrangement commences. 2.1.4 The Company must give the Employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 2.1.5 The Company or Employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: (a) by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or (b) if the Company and Employee agree in writing at any time. 2.2 Requests for flexible working arrangements 2.2.1 Requests for flexible working arrangements under section 65 of the Act will be managed in accordance with the procedure set out in Clause 6 of the Award as amended or replaced from time to time. 2.3 Consultation 2.3.1 In this term: Relevant Employees mean the Employees who may be affected by a change referred to in Clause 2.3.2. 2.3.2 This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \"Lead maintenance employees. 4e Trade - Electrical Dual Trades As per *Skills Matrix - At Discretion of Company <strong>Automation</strong>, Advanced More than 5 years' experience at 3e Level with post Trade and Trades, Cert-4 Post or Dual Trades Qualifications recognized and used by the Company.\"\n)", 
"Includes sachet operator and <strong>automated</strong> coin rolling machine.", 
"Ordinary Time Hours loadings replenishing; loading and unloading of vehicles; operation of computer 5.2.1.1 Ordinary Time Hours shall attract loadings equipment; operation of licensed or as per Table 5.2.1.1 for each actual hour non-licensed materials handling worked expressed as a percentage of the equipment; acceptance and despatch of Base Hourly Rate. goods; operation of <strong>automated</strong> storage 5.2.1.2 Where a Team Members Rostered Hours and retrieval systems; operation of Radio ends four (4) or more hours after the Frequency (RF) equipment; administrative beginning of a period attracting a higher work; on job training and assessment of percentage loading than their first rostered Team Members in Training, and other hour, then all hours shall be paid at the duties as directed. higher percentage loading. 5.1.3 Level 3 - Team Member/Team Example 1: Coordinator 1pm to 9pm shift on a Monday shall attract no loading up to 6pm and 120% from 6pm to 9pm The Company may appoint, at their for paid hours", 
"c(\"for the child and remains close by, usually in the same premises. 4.1.3 Award means the Children's Services Award 2010 or the Educational Services (Teachers) Award 2010 as varied from time to time. 4.1.4 Childcaremeans a program providing care, support supervision and development for children. 4.1.5 FWA means Fair Work Australia, as established by the Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth). 4.1.6 Spouse includes a de facto spouse, former de facto spouse whether or not such person is of the same sex as the Employee. 4.1.7 Union means United Voice. 4.1.8 Weeks pay means the ordinary time rate of pay for the Employee concerned as prescribed in Schedule 2 and Clause 2.3 4.1.9 Parties mean those set out in Clause 1.3. 4.1.10 Paid Leave means approved leave of absence from the worksite during which the Employee is paid by the Employer. 4.2 Consultation 4.2.1 This term applies if the employer: 4.2.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 4.2.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 4.2.2 Major change 4.2.2.1 For a major change referred to in paragraph 4.2.1.1: 4.2.2.1.1 the employer must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and 4.2.2.1.2 subclauses 4.2.3 to 4.2.9 apply. 4.2.3 The relevant employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this term. 4.2.4 If: 4.2.4.1 a relevant employee appoints, or relevant employees appoint, a representative for the purposes of consultation; and 4.2.4.2 the employee or employees advise the employer of the identity of the representative; 4.2.4.3 the employer must recognise the representative. 7\", \n\"employees concerned. 4.5.3.2 For the purposes of such discussion, the Employer must as soon as practicable provide in writing to the Employees concerned and the union, all relevant information about the proposed terminations, including; (i) the reasons for the proposed terminations; (ii) the number and categories of employees likely to be affected; (iii) the number of workers normally employed; and (iv) the period over which the terminations are likely to be carried out. 4.5.4 No Employer is required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be against the Employer's interests. 4.5.5 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.5.5.1 If the services of an Employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such an Employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.6. 4.5.5.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong>\"\n)", 
"GEOFABRICS AUSTRALASIA PTY LTD Wage Rate table As of 1st As of 1st NAME / CLASSIFICATION As of 1st RATE SET March March March 2021 2022 2023 2.50% 2.50% STRSUPY Stores Supervisor $ 37.9893 $ 38.9390 :39.9125 STR2IC Stores 2IC $ 32.1366 $ 32.9400 $ 33.7635 STRASST Storeperson $ 30.8368 $ 31.6077 $ 32.3979 ANCIL COORD Ancillaries Co-ordinator $ 35.3069 $ 36.1896 $ 37.0943 MAINTPLANN Maintenance Planner $ 45.5072 $ 46.6449 $ 47.8110 MAINTFITA Maintenance Fitter A 39.9631 40.9621 $ 41.9862 MAINTFITB Maintenance Fitter B $ 37.4677 $ 38.4044 $ 39.3645 MAINTELEC Electrical and <strong>Automation</strong> $ 45.5072 $ 46.6449 $ 47.8110 SPS Shift Supervisor $ 54.2777 $ 55.6346 $ 57.0255 2ICPROD Shift 2IC 46.1902 $ 47.3449 $ 48.5285 SFTOP Shift Operator $ 43.1824 $ 44.4833 $ 46.6400 SFTTR1 Trainee Operator Level 1[ 1- 8 wks ) 35.7770 36.6714 $ 37.5882 SFTTR2 Trainee Operator Level 2 [ 9 - 26 wks ] $ 39.3767 $ 40.3611 $ 41.3701 SFTOPBASER Shift Operator base rate $ 29.9961 $ 30.8998 $ 32.3979 GENOP Flex operator $", 
"c(\"INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 16.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\", \n\"(b) Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Employer is engaged must be given not less than three (3) months notice of termination.\")", 
"c(\"(vi) The number of shift positions performing scheduled maintenance at any one time in any unit (as per clause 24.1) will be subject to the STL discretion and based on manning competency and availability and is subject to the following: > The tools required to perform the work are in the EBA as listed in 17.1 > The parts required for the job are readily available The work is to be done within the scope as set out in 17.1 > The operator has the competency to carry out the task (vii) Jobs involving 6\\\" 300# piping should not form part of any planned, scheduled or tasked maintenance jobs but only be done in emergency circumstances consistent with the flange tightening procedure in GRAP 5.7.01. 18 Laboratory testing In general, equipment used in plant laboratories will be of the semi-<strong>automated</strong> type to minimise the complexity and time required to carry out tests.\", \n\"Final Draft_ Proposed Viva Energy Ops EA 2019 Attachment 5 - Consultation clause (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Viva Energy Ops EA 2014 (Final) 191014.docx (vii) Jobs involving 6\\\" 300# piping should not form part of any planned, scheduled or tasked maintenance jobs but only be done in emergency circumstances consistent with the flange tightening procedure in GRAP 5.7.01. 18 Laboratory testing In general, equipment used in plant laboratories will be of the semi-<strong>automated</strong> type to minimise the complexity and time required to carry out tests.\", \"Viva Energy Ops EA 2014 (Final) 191014.docx Attachment 5 - Consultation clause (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"c(\"(h) The employee can have a representative of their choice present at any stage of the above process if they wish. 2.2 Consultation and Notification of Intended Change This term applies if the Company: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees. 2.3 Major change (a) For a major change referred to in clause 2.2(a): (1) the company must notify the relevant employees of the decision to introduce the major change; and (2) clauses 2.3(b) to 2.3(h) apply.\", \n\"(D) Any dispute arising out of clause 3.4(c)(2) will be dealt with in accordance with the Dispute clause 2.1 of this Agreement. (3) Period of Notice (A) If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee shall be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 3.3 of this Agreement, (B) Provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the employer is engaged shall be given not less than three months notice of termination.\"\n)", 
"CLERICAL ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2015 4. 10.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy (a) If the services of an Employee are to be terminated due to redundancy the Employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.9.1(a) - Termination of employment (b) Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Employer is engaged must be given not less than three (3) months' notice of termination.",

"Provided that the Company will not be required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be inimical to the Company's interests. 29.3 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 29.3.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee will be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 29 of this Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like 19 of 29",

"c(\"The nature of the duties includes work of the following nature: 17.2.2(a) basic repetitive work on <strong>automated</strong> or single purpose machines or equipment, e.g. automatic filling 17.2.2(b) simple machine tinting 17.2.2(c) basic receipt, despatch and inventory work AT765560 Clause 17 (p. 1 of 9)\", \"The nature of the duties includes work of the following nature: 17.3.3(a) mill operation 17.3.3(b) dispersion operation 17.3.3(c) raw materials preparation 17.3.3(d) powder coating operations 17.3.3(e) resin manufacturing under supervision 17.3.3(f) operation of all materials handling equipment 17.3.3(g) tinting under supervision 17.3.3(h) routine production testing 17.3.3(i) elementary research and development work involving no formal training 17.3.3(j) make up operation 17.3.3(k) fully integrated <strong>automated</strong> filling systems (ie.\"\n)",

"employee whose employment has been terminated, provide the employee a written statement specifying the period of their employment and classification of or the type of work performed by the employee. 8.1.5 Payment in Lieu If the Company makes payments in lieu for all or any of the period of notice prescribed, the period for which such payment is made will be treated as service for the purposes of calculating any service related entitlements of the employee arising under this Agreement. 8.2 REDUNDANCY 8.2.1 Definitions 8.2.1.1 \"Redundancy\" in this clause means the loss of employment due to the Company no longer requiring the job the employee has been doing to be performed by anyone. 8.2.2 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, the employee will be given notice of termination as prescribed in clause 8.1.1, provided that, where the termination results from the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong>", 
"c(\"Consultation 7.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) is proposing to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Packaging methods include manual processes, semi-automatic and fully <strong>automated</strong> packaging equipment.\")", 
"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.00) Model consultation term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program. organisation, structure of <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.", 
"the reasons for the proposed terminations; (b) the number and categories of Employees likely to be affected; (c) the number of Employees normally employed; and (d) the period over which the terminations are likely to be carried out 21.2 Method of selection and procedure for redundancy In the first instance, the Employer shall call for volunteers from the area where surplus labour exists: 21.2.1 In the event of insufficient volunteers the Employer reserves the right to select Employees for redundancy. 21.2.2 In the event of excessive volunteers, the Employer will select the persons from that list of volunteers to be made redundant. 21.3 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 21.3.1 If the services of an Employee are to be terminated due to redundancy the Employer must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 20.1.1. 21.3.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong>", 
"Provided that the Employer will not be required to disclose confidential information, the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be inimical to the Employer's interests. 4.4.3 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.4.3.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such employee will be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 4.3 of this Agreement provided that employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Employer is engaged will be given not less than three (3) months notice of termination. 4.4.3.2 Should the Employer fail to give notice of termination as required in sub-clause 4.4.3.1 herein, the Employer will pay to that employee an amount calculated in accordance with the employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference", 
"NULL", "NULL", "CONSULTATION (a) This term applies if the Company: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.", 
"Where new technology or <strong>automation</strong> is introduced to the Pfizer Melbourne site, the required training will be provided to maintenance employees. 40.2 During the life of this Agreement, parties agree that the full transition of work centre change overs from maintenance to production will occur.", 
"c(\"Subject Matter 36 23 Additional Conditions of Employment Allowances 22 Annual Leave 2 31 Arrangement <strong>Automation</strong> 26 Board for Employees Temporarily Transferred 32B 27 Carers Leave Casual Employees 35 Compassionate Leave 48 Consultation 43 Continuity of Service for the purpose of the Divestment to OneSteel Manufacturing Pty Ltd 29 Contract of Hiring 16 Day Workers Temporarily on Night Work 7 Definitions 33 Employee Representative 40 Employee Representative Training Leave 20 Extra Rates not Cumulative 49 Flexibility Term 44 Flexible Remuneration 8 Hours of Work 19 Leading Hands 5 Locality 45 Long Service Leave 9 Meal Breaks 12 Minimum Payment of Time 4B Mobility of Labour Between the Company's Whyalla and Middleback Ranges sites 6 No Extra Claims 34 Notice Boards 10 Overtime 37 Parental Leave 1\", \n\"Clause 31 - <strong>Automation</strong> (a) Despite the provisions of Clause 30 of this Agreement, where the Company proposes to terminate the employment of an employee who has been employed by it for the preceding twelve months, on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of mechanisation or technological changes, it shall give the employee three month's notice of the termination of employment.\")", 
"NULL", "c(\"Technology and Continuous Improvement 13.4 The Company and employees will support and participate in the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> and other improvements in technology along with general continuous improvement methodologies.\", \"Schedule D Consultation Term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)", 
"The Company reserves the right to reject volunteers on the basis of operational need. 8.2.3 Should the Company not receive the required number of volunteers, the Company may implement involuntary redundancy process. 8.2.4 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, the employee will be given notice of termination as prescribed in clause 8.1.1, provided that, where the termination results from the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes, the employee will be given not less than three (3) months' notice of termination. 8.2.5 Notice to Centrelink Where a decision has been made to terminate the employment of employees on account of redundancy, the Company will notify Centrelink as soon as possible, giving relevant information including a written statement of the reason(s) for the termination(s), the number and categories of employees likely to be affected,", 
"NULL", "NULL", "(vi) The Company is committed to the five-week <strong>automation</strong>, electronic, electrical functions (AEE) course.", 
"39.4 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 39.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 38.2. 39.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the organisation must be given not less than three (3) months' notice of termination. 39.4.3 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 39.4.1 or 39.4.2 the employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.", 
"Further where a redundancy occurs as a result of the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> or other like 26", 
"Easy Work: Little physical exertion required e.g. picking parts from an extruder line, labeling containers, filling small epoxy tins while sitting, paperwork, operating an <strong>automated</strong> process such as the MPT filling machine or bulk liquid dosing to a mixer Some physical exertion required e.g. moving bags with a vacuum lifter, driving a forklift with frequent boarding Moderate Work: and alighting, man-handling empty drums for washing Hard Work: Heavy physical exertion over a sustained period e.g. cutting up an extrusions bulk batch, manually loading 25 kg bags into a mixer where there is no vacuum lifter, packing off a powders batch into 25 kg bags 2019 Kilsyth EA FINAL.docx 72", 
"OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY The Company reserves the right to introduce new technology, systems, processes and programs including but not limited to robotics/<strong>automation</strong>, new machinery, new equipment, warehousing software or computer systems which can contribute to increasing the operational efficiency and/or effectiveness of the business.", 
"24.1 Waiting Time Penalties An employee kept waiting for his/her wages on pay day, for other than circumstances beyond the control of the employer, for more than a quarter of an hour after the usual time of ceasing work shall be paid at overtime rates after that quarter-hour within a minimum of a quarter of an hour 24.2 Payment on Termination a) When notice is given of termination, all moneys due to the employee shall be paid at the time of termination. b) Where this is not practicable the employer shall have two working days to ensure monies are deposited to the Employee's nominated bank account unless advised by the Employee that the monies should be sent by cheque by registered post 24.3 Transition to Weekly Pays The company will be introducing time and attendance software that will introduce a new level of <strong>automation</strong> in the pay process.", 
"(iii) For these reasons the employees and the Unions agree to actively work in cooperation with the management of the Company at Camellia to identify and implement incremental continuous improvement initiatives which achieve their goals of increasing the productivity of manufacturing and distribution personnel to current worlds best practice by not only taking advantage of the latest developments in technology and production management systems (examples of which are <strong>automation</strong>, video and telecommunications as well as computerisation), but also improving the efficiency of existing technology and systems by targeting waste and downtime elimination.", 
"The employer is not required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which, when looked at objectively, would be against the employer's interests. 39.4 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 39.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 38.2. 39.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the organisation must be given not less than 3 months notice of termination. 39.4.3 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 39.4.1 or 39.4.2 the employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.", 
"NULL", "As such the parties are committed to pursue (through consultative processes) the introduction of the improvements into the Company operations that may include but not limited to the following practices <strong>Automation</strong> Multi Deck Loading/Unloading operations Technological Improvements 8.2 Levels of manning, equipment and methods of operation may be varied from time to time by the Company subject to safe work practices, improved technology, and new types of machinery or systems, customer service needs or for any other reason. 8.3 Nothing in this agreement shall impede the process of ongoing change to continuously improve the viability, efficiency and productivity of the Company. 8.4 The Company having made a decision that it intends to proceed with any significant change shall advise the MUA in writing and employees of the nature of the change, the reason for it, the timing of it, and any other relevant information.", 
"19.3 Training directed by Virgin Australia will not be rostered at the conclusion of a Tour of Duty. 19.4 Within three (3) Roster Periods of achieving <strong>automated</strong> system capability, a Team Member whose Home Base is different from a nominated local port at which they reside may request to be scheduled training at that local port subject to the following: (a) Virgin Australia offering the relevant training course at the Team Member's local port; (b) Virgin Australia having availability on the relevant training course at the Team Member's local port; and (c) Virgin Australia requiring the Team Member to undertake the training during the relevant time period that it is offered at the local port. 20 REPORTING 20.1 Information about which position you will report to at Virgin Australia will be outlined in your offer of employment or as directed from time to time.", 
"In addition to functions of a Level B employee, a Level C employee will Despatch internal and external resources effectively; Ensure sufficient field resources are available; Monitor <strong>automated</strong> systems and take appropriate corrective action if necessary; Provide periodic reports on activities; Provide leadership to lower level consultants where necessary Support positive and effective team environments Support RACQ way of doing business RACQ Assistance Contact Centre Enterprise Agreement 2018 - 2021 Page of 42 of 44", 
"c(\"INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE IN THE WORKPLACE 35.1 This term applies if the Employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"(g) Fault finding/root cause analysis (h) Knowledge of <strong>automated</strong> processes 47.3.\")", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "c(\"FOODMACH Inspired <strong>Automation</strong> Solutions Foodmach Pty.\", \"FOODMACH Inspired <strong>Automation</strong> Solutions pulletieing - depalletiaing - robotics - conveying Foodmach Pty.\")", 
"NULL", "NULL", "This flexibility is dependent on the type of product produced, e.g. jumbo rolls and introduction of <strong>automation</strong>.", 
"NULL", "Technological changes to the vessel including but not limited to <strong>automation</strong>. 17.3 Where a vessel has less than the normal complement of crew as covered by this Agreement, the vessel's operations shall not continue if the reduced crew numbers are in contravention of the provisions of National/International Legislation or applicable flag state requirements. 17.4 When a vessel covered by this Agreement is imported to work in the dredging industry the Company will, in accordance with past practice, invite one authorised employee representative to undertake a vessel inspection prior to the vessel's mobilisation.", 
"NULL", "Has completed their qualification in Certificate 4 - Industrial <strong>Automation</strong> or equivalent, and 3.", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "Duties in the Goods to Person (GTP) <strong>automation</strong> area .", 
"Knowledge of <strong>automated</strong> financial and accounting reporting systems.", 
"Easy Work: Little physical exertion required e.g. picking parts from an extruder line, labeling containers, filling small epoxy tins while sitting, paperwork, operating an <strong>automated</strong> process such as the MPT filling machine or bulk liquid dosing to a mixer Moderate Work: Some physical exertion required e.g. moving bags with a vacuum lifter, driving a forklift with frequent boarding and alighting, man-handling empty drums for washing Hard Work: Heavy physical exertion over a sustained period e.g. cutting up an extrusions bulk batch, manually loading 25 kg bags into a mixer where there is no vacuum lifter, packing off a powders batch into 25 kg bags 2024 Kilsyth EA (Changes Approved) 73", 
"NULL", "NULL", "c(\"15) CONSULTATION PROCEDURE (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"Tasks that a T/A would be expected to complete safely include (but are not limited to): - Complete tool change (removal & refit) of IML Tooling & Hot Runner, including safe operation of the overhead hoist - Changeover of Robot/<strong>Automation</strong> parts, as required for Job Change on any machine. - Disassembly & Reassembly of IML tooling either in workshop or whilst installed in machine: - Cavity removal & installation - Cleaning of tooling/cavities - Replacement of seals & O-rings - Troubleshooting of Production Issues (including but not limited to): - Conducting thorough Root Cause Analysis of issues to accurately determine the fault - Conduct pareto analysis of rejected tubs to establish defect or cavity trends - Analyse the results of pareto analysis & other factors to identify likely root causes for issue & eliminate unlikely causes - Label placement issues (correct adjustment of labels) - Identify issues with static on machine & - Troubleshooting basic pneumatic components Visy Packaging Pty\"\n)", 
"c(\"CLAUSE 4.3 INTRODUCTION OF CHANGE 4.3.1 This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\", \n\"No Employer is required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be against the Employer's interests. 4.7.4 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.7.4.1 If the services of an Employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such an Employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.6.1.1. 4.7.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Employee is engaged, must give not less than three months notice of termination. 4.7.4.3 Should the Employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 4.6.1.1 or 4.6.1.2 the Employer must pay to that Employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"The general obligation of employers should be no more than to give such employees and/or their chosen representatives, including the Union, an indication of the impending redundancy at the first reasonable opportunity, and to take such steps as must be reasonable to facilitate the obtaining by such employees of suitable alternative employment. 4.8.2(b) This clause does not apply where employment is terminated as a consequence of conduct that at common law justifies instant dismissal or in the case of casual employees or employees engaged for a specific period of time or for a specified task or tasks. 4.8.3 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.8.3(a) If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy the employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 4.7 - Termination of employment. 4.8.3(b) Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong>",

"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "Be able to construct and quote complex and/or non-<strong>automated</strong> fares and/or taxes and to issue all ticketing documentation including manual tickets as required Guest Services Level 4 (GS4) Work with limited guidance or direction Implement new/improved systems, as directed Maintain professional relationships with customers Assist in the co-ordination of staffing resources at operational areas Handle sensitive enquiries with tact and discretion Resolve customer complaints Research and collate data from various information sources Determine problems and potential problems, identify options for resolution and take corrective action Guest Services Level 5 (GS5) (Advanced) Where appropriate, oversee a small number of staff carrying out Guest Services duties with the aim of providing the best possible service within their control Providing direction, monitoring performance/service delivery and providing coaching/feedback to relevant team members VIRGIN AUSTRALIA GROUND CREW AGREEMENT 2018 25", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "c(\"The Company is required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which when looked at objectively, would be against the Company's interests. 4.3.4 Period of notice of termination on redundancy 4.3.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy that employee must be given notice of termination as stated by clause 4.2. 4.3.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Company is engaged must be given not less than three months notice of termination. 4.3.4.3 Should the Company fail to give notice of termination as required in 4.3.4.1 or 4.3.4.2 in this Agreement, the Company must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\", \n\"The Company is required to disclose confidential information the disclosure of which, when looked at objectively, would be against the Company's interests. 29.4 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 29.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by clause 3.4.1 of this Section of the agreement. 29.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Company of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes, in the industry in relation to which the Company is engaged, must be given not less than 3 months notice of termination. 29.4.3 Should the Company fail to give notice of termination as required in 29.3.1 or 29.3.2 the Company must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "c(\"DCE ELECTRICAL AND LEOPARD CONTROLS ENTERPRISE AGREEMENT 2021 46.7.1 an Employee must continue to perform his or her work as he or she would normally unless he or she has a reasonable concern about an imminent risk to his or her health or safety; and 46.7.2 an Employee must comply with a direction given by the Company to perform other available work at the same workplace, or at another workplace, unless: a. the work is not safe; or b. applicable occupational health and safety legislation would not permit the work to be performed; or C. the work is not appropriate for the Employee to perform; or d. there are other reasonable grounds for the Employee to refuse to comply with the direction. 46.8 The parties to the dispute agree to be bound by a decision made by Fair Work Commission in accordance with this term. 47 CONSULTATION 47.1 This term applies if the Company: 47.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure, or <strong>technology</strong>\", \n\"<strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or 47.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees.\")",

"NULL", "NULL", "(b) The following allowance will be paid from the first full pay period to commence on or after the dates set out below: Current 1-Jul-2017 Chemical Handling $38.79 $39.57 Allowance (c) The Company agrees to review <strong>automation</strong> opportunities. 18.7.", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "c(\"A Secretary (to take the minutes). 3.2 CONSULTATION 3.2.1 General (a) This clause applies if the Employer: (i) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>Employees</strong>; or (ii) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of Employees (b) The relevant Employees may appoint a representative for the purposes of the procedures in this clause, (c) Once an Employee has advised the Employer of the identity of their appointed representative, the Employer must recognise the representative for the purposes of consultation under this clause.\", \n\"TAYLORS WINES PTY LTD Taylors Wines Enterprise Agreement 2015 4.6.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the Employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the industry in relation to which the Employer is engaged must be given not less than three (3) months' notice of termination. 4.6.4.3 Should the Employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 4.6.4.1 or 4.6.4.2 in this Agreement, the Employer must pay to that Employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.\"\n)", 
"c(\"Technology and Continuous Improvement 13.4 The Company and employees will support and participate in the introduction of <strong>automation</strong> and other improvements in technology along with general continuous improvement methodologies.\", \"Schedule D Consultation Term (1) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or (b) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)",

"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "39.4 Period of Notice of Termination on Redundancy 39.4.1 If the services of an employee are to be terminated due to redundancy, such an employee must be given notice of termination as prescribed by Clause 38.2. 39.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of <strong>automation</strong> or other like technological changes in the organisation must be given not less than 3 months notice of termination. 39.4.3 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 39.4.1 or 39.4.2 the employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the difference between the notice given and that required to be given.",

"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "8.1.5 Roll out and embedding of the <strong>automated</strong> e-workflow document management system. 8.1.6 Culture change and transition program, including reengineering priority processes. 8.1.7 Establishment of a PTV/Employee/Union Reference Group to inform the development of a strategy to address actual or perceived employee workload and well-being concerns.", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "Classifications 13.1 All Employees covered by this Agreement must be classified according to the structure set out in this clause. 13.2 Employers must advise their Employees in writing of their classification and of any changes to their classification from time to time. 13.3 The classification by the Employer must be according to the skill level or levels required to be exercised by the Employee in order to carry out the principal functions of the employment as determined by the Employer. 13.4 Employees will be classified in accordance with the following classification structure: (a) Introductory Agency Employee; (b) Seller; or (c) Cash Control Operator. 13.4.1 Indicative tasks required of a Seller include: (a) sell and pay bets using the <strong>automated</strong> ticketing equipment; (b) ensure individual cash float is maintained and correct; (c) balance own terminal at designated times of the day; (d) provide customer service (e.g. updating information on racing, products, equipment, etc.); (e) keep", 
"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", 
"NULL", "Northern Co-Operative Meat Company Ltd Maintenance and Services Enterprise Agreement 2017 The employer or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: 7.5.1 by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or 7.5.2 if the employer and employee agree in writing - at any time. 8 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 8.1 This clause applies if the employer: 8.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 8.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.",

"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "c(\"The formula is developed by the service delivery framework and this is reviewed annually. 8.2 Service delivery demands and service resource allocations are monitored on a quarterly basis utilising the <strong>automated</strong> Workload Estimation Tool with the aim of managing distribution and/or re-distribution of available resources whilst balancing client community access with reasonable staff workloads.\", \"Schedule 2.3 Model consultation term (regulation 2.09) Model consultation term ) This term applies if the employer: (a) has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or ) proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.\"\n)",

"NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "NULL", "Northern Co-Operative Meat Company Ltd Maintenance and Services Enterprise Agreement 2019 7.4 The employer must give the employee a copy of the individual flexibility arrangement within 14 days after it is agreed to. 7.5 The employer or employee may terminate the individual flexibility arrangement: 7.5.1 by giving no more than 28 days written notice to the other party to the arrangement; or 7.5.2 if the employer and employee agree in writing - at any time. 8 CONSULTATION REGARDING MAJOR WORKPLACE CHANGE 8.1 This clause applies if the employer: 8.1.1 has made a definite decision to introduce a major change to production, program, organisation, structure or <strong>technology</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>relation</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>its</strong> <strong>enterprise</strong> <strong>that</strong> <strong>is</strong> <strong>likely</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>have</strong> <strong>a</strong> <strong>significant</strong> <strong>effect</strong> <strong>on</strong> <strong>the</strong> <strong>employees</strong>; or 8.1.2 proposes to introduce a change to the regular roster or ordinary hours of work of employees.",
